title: Safety Compliance Engineer / Civil Engineer (Construction) - Remote (UK/Ireland Experience Required)
description:
Please follow the application instructions below.



About PropFix.ie



PropFix.ie is Ireland's trusted property maintenance specialist, delivering comprehensive solutions across the construction and renovation sectors. Our services range from general maintenance and office fit-outs to full-scale renovations, house extensions, and specialized installations including doors, flooring, partitions, and MEP (Mechanical, Electrical & Plumbing) systems.



With decades of industry experience, we are committed to delivering high-quality workmanship and reliable service to both residential and commercial clients nationwide. Our strong reputation is built on attention to detail, safety compliance, and our ability to bring client visions to life while maintaining the highest industry standards.



Role Overview



We are seeking a detail-oriented Health & Safety / Compliance Coordinator to support our growing operations. This role focuses on ensuring all construction projects adhere to strict health & safety standards, regulatory requirements, and internal compliance procedures.



The successful candidate will play a key role in preparing safety documentation, supporting project teams, and maintaining accurate compliance records. You will work closely with project managers, site teams, and stakeholders to ensure safe and efficient project delivery.



Key Responsibilities

Health & Safety Documentation



Prepare Risk Assessments and Method Statements (RAMS) to support safe work practices across all projects

Develop Construction Stage Plans outlining safety procedures, responsibilities, and control measures

Assist in preparing PSDP (Preliminary Safety and Health Plans) and PSCS (Construction Stage Safety Plans) documentation

Ensure all documentation aligns with Irish health & safety and construction regulations



Compliance & Record Management



Maintain, review, and update all health & safety and compliance documentation

Ensure all records are accurate, current, and audit-ready

Organize and maintain safety files, reports, and compliance records for easy access



Project Coordination & Support



Coordinate with project managers, contractors, and stakeholders to ensure safety requirements are implemented

Support project teams in meeting compliance standards throughout all project stages

Assist with general safety administration and compliance-related tasks



Monitoring & Reporting



Conduct video reviews of site activities and training materials to ensure compliance with safety standards

Produce detailed reports based on site Health & Safety audits

Identify potential risks and support the implementation of corrective actions



Project Scheduling & Systems



Use 
 to manage project timelines, tasks, and compliance tracking

Support workflow coordination and ensure timely completion of safety-related deliverables



Required Skills & Experience

Technical Requirements



Experience preparing RAMS and construction safety documentation

Knowledge of PSDP and PSCS processes (preferred)

Familiarity with Irish health & safety regulations in construction

Experience managing compliance documents and audit records

Experience using project management tools such as 
 (or similar platforms)



Professional Skills



Strong attention to detail and organizational skills

Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines efficiently

Analytical mindset with problem-solving capabilities

Ability to work independently in a remote environment



What We Offer



Competitive salary package

Remote working flexibility

Opportunity to work on Irish and international construction projects

Supportive and collaborative team environment

Career growth within a fast-growing company

Exposure to diverse projects across construction, renovation, and compliance



Application Instructions

Please complete the attached application form and submit it along with your CV:

title: Monday.com Specialist (Migration + Automation QA & Launch Owner)
description:
We’re looking for an experienced **
 specialist** to step in, audit our current setup, and confidently take ownership of launching our new system.



We are in the process of migrating **two systems from Zoho to 
**. One system (a smaller brand) is already fully migrated, live, and working well. The second system is in progress, and we need someone to ensure everything is properly configured, connected, and ready before we fully transition.



**Current Tech Stack:**



* 
 (primary CRM/project management)

* Zoho (legacy system being phased out)

* Zapier (core integration layer)

* SimplyBook (in-person workshop registrations)

* Demio (webinar registrations)



**What We Need:**

Before we turn off Zoho automations and go live with 
, we need a detail-oriented expert to:



* Audit and double-check our 
 setup

* Review all workflows, automations, and integrations

* Ensure Zapier connections are functioning correctly end-to-end

* Validate data flow from SimplyBook and Demio into 

* Identify gaps, errors, or risks in the system

* Clean up and optimize where needed

* Take full ownership of the **launch process**

* Give us confidence that everything will run smoothly post-transition



**Ideal Candidate:**



* Proven experience with **
 (advanced workflows & automations)**

* Strong experience with **Zapier integrations**

* Familiarity with **CRM migrations (especially from Zoho)**

* Detail-oriented and proactive problem solver

* Able to think through systems, not just tasks

* Comfortable taking ownership and making recommendations



**Nice to Have:**



* Experience with SimplyBook and/or Demio

* Background in marketing funnels or lead management systems



**Opportunity for Long-Term Role:**

If this project goes well and we work well together, there is strong potential to transition into a **full-time role**. This could include:



* Ongoing project management

* Keeping 
 updated with workshop and webinar schedules

* Supporting light digital marketing efforts

* Handling other small but important digital tasks across the business



**Project Scope:**

This is a short-term, high-impact project with potential for ongoing work if it’s a strong fit.



If you’ve successfully handled similar migrations and can step in to ensure a smooth, error-free launch, we’d love to hear from you.

Make revisions based on detailed feedback

Prepare final files for print (high-resolution, properly formatted)

title: AI Video Production VA (No Experience Needed)
description:
We're Hiring!

AI Video Production Assistant

Full-Time | Remote | Long-Term Only



Hi! We run a fast-growing network of YouTube channels, publishing daily content across multiple niches. We're looking for someone who's ready to fully commit — not just to a job, but to a workflow, a team, and a long-term journey of growth.



This is a production role, not a creative one. You'll follow a defined workflow to take video concepts from start to finish — scripting, video creation, and publishing — using a suite of AI tools we'll train you on. The learning curve is real. The first weeks will require patience, focus, and a willingness to put in the hours to truly master the process. But once you do, this becomes a stable, long-term role with room to grow.



We want someone who sees this as more than a job — someone who takes pride in improving, hits their targets consistently, and grows with us over time. If you're looking for something quick or easy, this isn't it. If you're looking for something meaningful and sustainable, keep reading.



What We're Looking For

• Available full-time (8 hrs/day — this role demands your full attention)

• Willing to invest serious time upfront to learn the workflow properly

• Strong attention to detail — accuracy in publishing matters every single day

• Self-motivated and able to work independently without being chased

• Comfortable learning new browser-based AI tools quickly

• Reliable internet and responsive during working hours

• Good written English

• Fully committed long-term — we're building a team, not filling a temp slot



Nice to Have

• YouTube or content publishing experience

• Prior use of AI tools (writing, image, or video generation)

• Virtual assistant or operations background



Compensation

Base: $300 USD/month + potential bonuses!

Performance review after 2 months with potential increase.

All tools are paid for — you don't spend a cent.



To Apply

Send a short message starting with the word 'DEDICATED' (to confirm you've read this). Include: a brief intro and where you're based, your availability and timezone, any relevant experience, and why you're ready to commit fully to a long-term, process-driven role.



Please only apply if this would be your one and only focus. We need someone all in — not someone fitting this in between other things.

title: Ads Manager — Creative Partner & Meta Strategist
description:
(Not your average ads role. Please read before applying.)



We’re looking for someone who eats, breathes, sleeps, and obsesses over ads — not because they want the results, but because they genuinely love the craft. The kind of person who sees a scroll-stopping hook in the wild and immediately screenshots it. Who reads ad breakdowns for fun. Who gets fired up talking about creative angles the way other people talk about their favorite shows.

If that’s you — keep reading. If it’s not — this role will drain you, and we want better for both of us.



Who We Are



We’re a brand on a mission: every product we sell is designed to give people emotional freedom. This isn’t a tagline for us — it’s the reason we show up. We believe the world’s emotional health matters, and every ad, caption, and piece of copy we put out represents the movement we stand for and the people we stand with.

Our culture runs on energy, creativity, and exceptional standards. We’re high achievers who also have fun. We brainstorm hard, test fast, and care deeply about the words we choose — because how we say something matters as much as the sale itself.



Who You Are



• Obsessed with ads in the best way. You don’t just run campaigns — you study them, love them, and light up when you talk about a hook that worked.

• A Meta expert with real stories. You can walk me through specific clients you’ve supported, the creative you tested, what flopped, what won, and what you learned. No vague case studies — actual receipts.

• Creative as much as strategic. You can build a media buying plan and write a hook that stops the scroll. Some weeks I’ll lead the creative, some weeks you will — this is a partnership, not a hand-off.

• Fluent in HYROS. You already know and actively use HYROS for attribution — not something you’ll learn on the job.

• AI-literate, but never AI-generic. You stay on top of current AI tools and use them to sharpen brainstorming and analytics — but you put your whole heart into the final words. We never publish soulless AI copy.

• Energized by brainstorming. You come to every week with new ideas, fresh hooks, and a genuine excitement to try things. You don’t wait to be asked.

• Aligned with the mission. You care about the emotional health of the people on the other side of the ad. You understand that our community isn’t a “target audience” — they’re humans we’re helping toward emotional freedom.

What You’ll Do



• Own strategy, launch, and daily management of our Meta ad campaigns

• Bring new creative angles, hooks, and test ideas to every weekly brainstorm

• Collaborate with me on creative — sometimes leading, sometimes supporting

• Use HYROS to track true attribution and make confident scaling decisions

• Leverage AI tools for ideation and analytics — while keeping every final word human, intentional, and on-brand

• Protect the voice of our brand as fiercely as you chase the ROAS

What to Include in Your Application



• A real story: a client you supported, what you tried, what worked, what didn’t, and why

• An ad or hook you’ve written recently that you’re proud of — and a quick note on why it worked

• How you currently use HYROS in your workflow

• One AI tool or trend you’re genuinely excited about right now and how you’d use it for us

One More Thing



If you’re the kind of ads person who gets a little buzz in your chest when a test you believed in starts winning — we want to meet you. Bring your energy, your ideas, and your heart. We’ll match it.

title: Recruiting Manager
description:
About MZMO People Partners (MZMO)

MZMO is a women-led boutique recruiting firm dedicated to partnering with venture capital and private equity firms. We specialize in retained searches to foster deep client relationships and deliver an exceptional candidate experience.

By leveraging data-driven insights and incorporating operational excellence into our practice, we tailor each engagement to meet the unique needs of our clients. Our core values include integrity, accountability, continuous learning, clear communication, mental health, and giving back to our communities. We are committed to doing right by our clients, supporting our team, and maintaining a quality-over-quantity approach to provide focused and effective recruiting solutions.

Recruiting Manager

MZMO is seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Recruiting Manager to join our team. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a dynamic environment and excels at managing multiple projects simultaneously. As a key contributor, you will not only source top-tier talent for a variety of clients but also coordinate the end-to-end recruitment process. This includes scheduling interviews, maintaining clear communication between candidates and clients, and ensuring a seamless experience for all parties involved. We are seeking exceptionally bright and reliable individuals who want to work in a LGBTQ+ friendly environment.

Key Responsibilities

Talent Sourcing: Using Boolean Search strategies to proactively identify and engage with potential candidates through various channels (i.e. LinkedIn, Sales Navigator, E-mail), ensuring a rich pipeline of talent for our clients

Project Management: Effectively oversee multiple talent sourcing projects concurrently, ensuring strategies are adjusted as needed to achieve weekly goals. Maintain accurate and up-to-date data to track progress and drive informed decision-making.

Candidate Engagement: Build and maintain strong relationships with potential candidates, communicating proactively to maintain good rapport and provide a great experience

Interview Coordination: Interacting with both clients and candidates to organize and schedule interviews across various platforms, ensuring a prompt and smooth experience.

Data Analysis and Reporting: Track and analyze outreach metrics such as response rates, relevancy and candidate engagement to identify trends and proactively address challenges. Use data insights to collaborate with clients and internal teams, ensuring realistic but ambitious weekly goals are set and met.



Ideal Qualifications

Experience: At least 3 years of experience in Talent Acquisition, with a proven track record of managing multiple projects and sourcing high-quality candidates.

Communication Skills: Exceptional verbal and written communication abilities.

Organizational Skills: Highly organized with a keen attention to detail, capable of juggling multiple projects without compromising quality.

Intellectual Curiosity: A strong desire to learn and grow, coupled with intellectual curiosity about various industries and roles.

Systems: Comfort with Google Suite, Slack, 
, LinkedIn, Octopus, E-mail Marketing





What Sets Us Apart

Growth Opportunities: We are committed to your personal and professional development, supporting your journey to become the best version of yourself.

Flexibility: We prioritize output over input, fostering a flexible and inclusive work environment.

Culture: LGBTQ Friendly & 2 paid hours of Community Service per month



Tactical Stuff

Hours: 40 hours a week

Compensation: $6 per hour with opportunity for promotions.

Commission Per Search: $100 - $700 

 

Application Process

To apply for the Recruiting Manager role, please follow these steps:



- Submit Your Resume and LinkedIn Profile

 your resume and LinkedIn profile to 
 with the subject line: Recruiting Manager Candidate.



Include a Video Introduction

- Record a brief video (up to 2 minutes) introducing yourself. Highlight your background, experience, and enthusiasm for this role. (Upload the video to YouTube, Loom, or Vimeo, and include the link in your
).



Write a Quick Note

- Tell me about the hardest recruiting project you've ever worked on. What made it challenging and what you did to overcome the challenge.

title: Recruitment Coordinator
description:
Overview

We are seeking a proactive and highly organized Recruitment Coordinator to support our talent acquisition team and ensure a seamless experience for candidates and hiring teams. This role is critical to keeping our hiring process running smoothly, from scheduling interviews to managing candidate communications and supporting reporting and recruiting operations.



Key Responsibilities

- Candidate Sourcing: We are obsessed with finding highly qualified and relevant leads across our various positions. You will be leading outreach, follow up and sourcing campaigns on up to 4 roles at one time. 

- Interview Coordination: Schedule phone, video, and onsite interviews across multiple time zones; liaise with candidates and internal stakeholders to confirm logistics.



- Candidate Experience: Serve as the primary point of contact for candidates throughout the interview process; ensure timely communication and a white-glove experience.



- ATS Management: Maintain up-to-date candidate records in the applicant tracking system (ATS); post and update job descriptions as needed.



- Reporting & Metrics: Assist in preparing weekly recruiting reports and pipeline summaries; track key hiring metrics like time-to-fill and candidate conversion.



- Hiring Team Support: Coordinate feedback collection, manage interviewer guides, and ensure timely debriefs post-interview.



- Onboarding Prep: Collaborate with People Ops or HR to ensure a smooth handoff from offer acceptance to onboarding.



- Process Improvement: Identify opportunities to improve candidate flow, communication, and team coordination.



Qualifications

- 1–3 years of experience in a recruiting or administrative coordination role

- Exceptional organizational and time management skills

- Strong communication skills, both written and verbal

- Comfortable working in a fast-paced, dynamic environment

- Familiarity with ATS platforms (e.g., Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby) and scheduling tools (e.g., Calendly, GoodTime) a plus

- Detail-oriented with a service mindset and a passion for delivering excellent candidate experience



Preferred Attributes

- Prior experience supporting multiple recruiters or hiring managers

- Experience working in a startup or high-growth environment

- Ability to handle confidential information with discretion

- A self-starter with a sense of ownership and urgency



If interested, please send an
 to: 
 with the subject: Recruiting Coordinator. Tell me about your coordinating experience and the hardest coordination project you've ever worked on.

title: SEO Specialist Onsite or WFH
description:

  ***Off-Page SEO

- Solid understanding of content creation, blogging, and SEO copywriting.

- Knowledge of Press Release distribution and optimization.

- Familiarity with local SEO, multi-location SEO, and E-E-A-T principles.

How to Apply:



Send your CV and portfolio to 
 with the subject line:

“Application – SEO Specialist [Your Name]”

title: Executive Assistant
description:
Reports To: Senior Executive Assistant

Location: Remote — must work in Pacific or Mountain Time Zone



The Executive Assistant provides high-level administrative support to the Chief Strategy Officer (CSO), Chief Information Officer (CIO), SVP, Client Services & Portfolio Management and Chief Compliance and Privacy Officer. This role requires agility in navigating shifting priorities within a dynamic, fast-paced environment, while exercising discretion, organization, and attention to detail. In addition to direct executive support, the Executive Assistant partners closely with the Senior Executive Assistant to drive alignment, coordination, and seamless support across the leadership team. The role also plays an integral part in strengthening cross-team collaboration within the Leadership Teams through planning and execution of key meetings, initiatives, and engagement events. 



• Oversee complex calendar management, coordinating internal and external meetings across multiple time zones, and accommodating last-minute changes and urgent business needs as required.

• Coordinate travel arrangements, itineraries, and on-the-ground logistics, ensuring seamless transitions between meetings, events, and locations.

• Serve as a professional point of contact between executives, internal teams, and external stakeholders.

• Support the planning and execution of executive and leadership events, including venue coordination, catering, travel, and onsite logistics, ensuring smooth delivery and professional, engaging participant experience.

• Process and reconcile expense reports in compliance with company policies.

• Collaborate with the Senior Executive Assistant to align priorities, share knowledge, and ensure consistent support across Executive and Senior Leadership teams.

• Maintain and update confidential records, files, and databases.

• Support the Senior Executive Assistant by helping maintain and evolve the EA playbook and standardized practices to ensure seamless, consistent executive support, and play a role in fostering an engaged EA community.

• Contribute to special projects and initiatives in support of ELT members, working closely with the Senior Executive Assistant to ensure timely progress and successful outcomes.

• Partner with the Senior Executive Assistant on Phoenix office operations, including supply ordering, hoteling office reservations, and logistical oversight, to ensure the office space runs smoothly and efficiently.

• Serve as a reliable backup to the Senior Executive Assistant, stepping in to ensure continuity of executive and leadership support during absences or periods of high demand.



Key Competencies:

• Executive Support Excellence

• Calendar, Travel, and Meeting Management 

• Adaptability and Agility in Managing Change and Shifting Priorities

• Effective Multitasking in a Fast-Paced Environment

• Communication & Professional Correspondence

• Collaboration and Relationship Building

• Strategic Prioritization & Problem Solving

• Technology & Systems Proficiency

title: High Capacity Executive Assistant
description:
OVERVIEW



We are looking for a sharp, proactive Virtual Executive Assistant to support a high-level executive with day-to-day operations. This is a part-time remote role (20 hours per week, Monday to Friday, approximately 1:00 PM to 5:00 PM Eastern Time). The right person is organized, tech-savvy, and thrives on keeping things moving without being told what to do next.



This is not a data entry role. This is not a "check the box" role. You will be the executive's right hand, keeping their world running smoothly while they focus on high-level strategy. If that excites you, keep reading.





WHO YOU'LL BE SUPPORTING



The executive you'll be working with wears a lot of hats, and all of them move fast.



- She leads a top-performing real estate team operating across the GTA, consistently ranking in the top 1% of the market. That means a high volume of transactions, team coordination, client-facing communications, and operational decisions happening every single day.

- She runs a large and growing professional community with hundreds of members, including flagship events, sponsorship relationships, content initiatives, and ongoing member engagement. Think event logistics, sponsor follow-ups, attendee communications, and post-event workflows.

- She is a recognized brand and thought leader in her industry, which means a steady stream of content creation, speaking engagements, social media presence, collaboration requests, and partnership opportunities that need to be managed and prioritized.



The volume is real. On any given day, you could be coordinating a meeting with a client, following up on a deliverable from a vendor, triaging a packed inbox, and making sure a community event timeline stays on track. If that sounds overwhelming, this isn't the role for you. If it sounds like exactly the kind of challenge you want, keep reading.





SCHEDULE AND LOGISTICS



- Hours: 20 hours per week (part-time)

- Schedule: Monday to Friday, approximately 1:00 PM to 5:00 PM ET

- Location: Fully remote

- Reports to: Executive directly





CORE RESPONSIBILITIES



Calendar and Meeting Coordination

- Manage and maintain the executive's calendar across multiple time zones and priorities

- Schedule, reschedule, and confir
etings with internal and external contacts

- Prepare meeting agendas, send pre-meeting materials, and ensure follow-ups are captured

- Proactively identify and resolve scheduling conflicts before they become problems



 Management

- Monitor, triage, and organize the executive's inbox daily

- Draft, edit, and send
s on behalf of the executive in a professional, on-brand tone

- Flag urgent items and ensure nothing falls through the cracks

- Maintain a clean, organized inbox system (labels, folders, filters)



Task and To-Do List Management

- Keep running task lists organized and up to date using project management tools (e.g., Asana, Notion, or similar)

- Track priorities and deadlines across multiple workstreams

- Provide daily or weekly summaries of outstanding items and upcoming deadlines

- Ensure the executive always knows what needs attention and what's been handled



Accountability and Follow-Up

- Track commitments, promises, and deadlines made by internal tea
mbers, vendors, and external contacts

- Send timely follow-ups and reminders to ensure deliverables are completed on time

- Escalate missed deadlines or at-risk items to the executive with context and recommended next steps

- Maintain a system for tracking who owes what and by when



General Executive Support

- Prepare documents, presentations, and reports as needed

- Coordinate travel arrangements and itineraries

- Research topics, vendors, or contacts as assigned

- Handle ad hoc administrative tasks and special projects





REQUIREMENTS



- Minimum 2+ years of experience as a virtual assistant or executive assistant

- Exceptional written communication skills (you will be tested on
 drafting and tone)

- Highly proficient with modern tech tools: Google Workspace, Slack, Asana (or similar PM platforms), Zoom, and CRM systems

- Comfortable with and actively using AI platforms (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) to increase efficiency

- Strong organizational skills with a proven ability to manage multiple priorities without dropping the ball

- Self-starter who takes initiative and anticipates needs rather than waiting for direction

- Professional, clear, and concise communicator who can represent the executive with confidence

- Reliable, punctual, and available during the scheduled hours (1:00 PM to 5:00 PM ET, Monday to Friday)





NICE TO HAVE



- Experience supporting executives in real estate, sales, or fast-paced team environments

- Familiarity with community management, event coordination, or membership-based organizations

- Experience with social media scheduling tools and content workflows

- Comfort with building and maintaining SOPs and process documentation





BEFORE YOU APPLY, READ THIS.



We respect your time, so we're going to be upfront. This posting is intentionally detailed because we're looking for someone specific. If you're sending the same application to 50 jobs today, this probably isn't the one for you.



Only apply if:



- You actually read this entire posting (we'll know if you didn't)

- You can commit to 1:00 PM to 5:00 PM Eastern Time, Monday to Friday, consistently. Not "flexibly." Consistently.

- You have real experience managing an executive's calendar, inbox, and tasks. Not "I'm a quick learner" experience. Actual experience.

- You're energized (not intimidated) by supporting someone who runs a business, a community, and a personal brand simultaneously

- You can write a professional
 that sounds like a human, not a template. Tone matters here.

- You use AI tools in your workflow already and can give a specific example of how

- You're comfortable being the person who follows up, nudges, and holds people accountable. Politely, but firmly.

- You don't need to be micromanaged. You see what needs to happen and you do it.



Do not apply if:



- You're looking for a role where you wait to be told what to do

- You're not available during the required hours

- You can't provide examples of past EA or VA work

- You struggle with juggling multiple priorities in a fast-moving environment





HOW TO APPLY



If you're still reading, good. That's already a sign. Your application is your first test. How you write it, how closely you follow the instructions, and how clearly you communicate will tell us everything we need to know about how you'd perform in this role.



Send your application with the following. Every item is required.



1. Subject Line

Use this exact subject line: "EA Application - [Your Name] - I Read the Whole Thing"



2. Your Resume

Attached as a PDF.



3. Three Short Answers (in the body of your
, not as an attachment)

Answer each of these in 2 to 4 sentences. We're not looking for essays. We're looking at how you write, how you think, and whether you actually read this posting.



a) Based on what you read in this posting, describe in your own words what a typical day in this role might look like.

b) Give a specific example of a time you followed up with someone who missed a deadline. What did you say, and what happened?

c) Name one AI tool you use regularly and explain exactly how it helps you get your work done faster or better.



4. Writing Sample

Draft a short
 for the following scenario (include it in the body of your application, below your short answers):



Scenario: A vendor promised to deliver a final version of a project by last Friday. It's now Wednesday and you haven't received it. The executive needs it by end of day Thursday for a presentation. Write the follow-up
 to the vendor on behalf of the executive.



This tells us how you handle tone, urgency, and professionalism in a real situation.



A few things we'll notice:

- Did you follow every instruction above?

- Does your writing sound like you, or like it was copied from a template (or generated by AI without any personal touch)?

- Can you communicate clearly and concisely, or do you over-explain?

- Did your answer to question (a) reflect what this posting actually says, or something generic?



Applications that are incomplete, do not follow these instructions, or are clearly mass-submitted will not be reviewed.





HIRING PROCESS



Candidates who make it past the application stage can expect:



1. Application review (including subject line and instructions check)

2.
 communication skills test (timed, practical scenario)

3. Tech and AI proficiency assessment

4. Video interview with the executive

5. Paid trial period





If you're the kind of person who gets satisfaction from a clean inbox, a perfectly managed calendar, and making sure nothing slips through the cracks, we want to hear from you.

title: Photo Retoucher
description:
Photo Retoucher





Title: Photo Retoucher — Two Positions Open (Beauty & Lookbook) — Long-Term Remote Work







We're a US-based retouching studio looking for skilled retouchers for long-term remote work. We have two positions open — one for a high-end beauty specialist, one for a strong lookbook and commercial retoucher. Read both before applying — we want to match you to the right role.



We do high-end beauty and fashion retouching as well as E-Commerse Lookbook and more for US and International brands and photographers. Quality is the standard, but speed is needed.







Position 1 — High-End Beauty Retoucher



For retouchers with genuine expertise in high-end beauty work: frequency separation, dodge and burn, careful light work, color grading, micro-detail preservation. Skin that retains texture. Work you're proud to put in a portfolio.



Requirements:



3+ years of high-end beauty retouching experience — beauty specifically, not just general photo editing

Portfolio with before-and-after examples on beauty or fashion work — skin that looks real, not plastic

Deep Photoshop proficiency: frequency separation, dodge & burn, advanced masking, color grading

Good written English and reliable internet and equipment



Compensation: $1,500–$2,000/month USD (ongoing, based on experience and output quality). We start with a short paid test — images tailored to your background and skill level.







Position 2 — Lookbook & Commercial Retoucher



For professional retouchers with a strong background in e-commerce lookbook, fashion catalog, or commercial photography editing. Clean, consistent, client-ready work delivered at a professional pace.



Requirements:



3+ years of professional retouching experience at a commercial level

Portfolio demonstrating clean, consistent lookbook or commercial work

Solid Photoshop proficiency: color correction, skin cleanup, compositing basics

Good written English and reliable internet and equipment



Compensation: $750–$1,200/month USD (ongoing, based on experience and output quality). We start with a short paid test — images tailored to your background and skill level.







How This Works



We start every new hire with a paid test — a small set of images selected for your background. We're not trying to waste your time, we're trying to see what you can actually do. If the work fits, we move to an ongoing monthly arrangement at the agreed rate. We pay reliably, give clear and specific feedback, and treat the people we work with professionally. Communication is async-friendly — primarily via
 and
, no mandatory call schedule.







To Apply



Send us:



Which position you're applying for

A link to your portfolio — before/after pairs preferred

Two or three sentences on your retouching background and what you do best



We respond to every application that fits.

title: TECHNICAL SUPPORT ENGINEER (INTEGRATIONS, QA, APIs) - US HOURS | G2 AWARD-WINNING US COMPANY
description:
Remote (Philippines) | Full-Time | Long-Term Role



You’re not looking to gig hop — you’re looking to build a career with a high-performing US-based team.



If you’re strong at troubleshooting, clear communication, and taking ownership, this role will feel right.



We’re hiring a Technical Support Engineer to help us solve real problems, improve systems, and grow into deeper technical responsibility over time.



We’re hiring a Technical Support Engineer to help us:



* resolve real customer issues

* improve QA and release confidence

* support go-lives and integrations

* reduce unnecessary interruptions to development

* strengthen how support, operations, and development work together



This is not entry-level support.

This is not a pure QA role.

This is not a junior developer role.



This is a hands-on technical role for someone who can work through ambiguity, think clearly, and move problems toward resolution without constant direction.



You do not need to be a full software engineer. But you do need to be comfortable with technical systems, structured data, debugging, and learning fast.



This role is a strong fit for someone from:



* technical support

* implementation support

* support engineering

* QA

* technical operations



ABOUT TOKEN OF TRUST



Token of Trust is a G2-recognized, award-winning platform that helps regulated ecommerce businesses verify consumer identities and comply with excise tax and reporting requirements.



We operate at the intersection of ecommerce, compliance, and trust — solving real-world problems for businesses that cannot afford to get it wrong.



We are a small, high-performing team. We value ownership, focus, continuous improvement, and delivering real results.



We are building a company where strong contributors can grow into deeper technical ownership over time — not just do a job, but become increasingly valuable as the company scales.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN



Technical Support Leadership (Primary Focus)



You will become the primary technical support person assigned to the Operations team for issues that require real troubleshooting discipline.



You will:



* triage, reproduce, and drive resolution of technical support issues

* distinguish between user error, configuration issues, integration problems, and product defects

* gather the right evidence before escalating to Development

* build and improve technical support playbooks so recurring issues can be delegated effectively

* personally drive challenging, ambiguous, or business-critical technical issues to closure

* communicate clearly with customers and internal teams during investigations

* become the trusted technical point of contact for operational issues that require strong judgment



QA and Release Confidence



You will be among the first to test new features and functionality so we can catch issues earlier and release with greater confidence.



You will:



* execute regression testing on key product flows

* write clean, reproducible bug reports with useful technical detail

* validate fixes before production release

* help reduce production surprises through disciplined testing and clear coverage

* build confidence that new functionality works across important use cases

* over time, help automate portions of regression testing and release validation



Go-Lives and Integration Enablement



You will help bring clients live successfully and, over time, act as the technical point person on go-lives and integration efforts.



You will:



* assist with client go-lives and implementation tasks

* configure plugins and integrations across platforms such as WordPress, WooCommerce, Shopify, and BigCommerce

* in some cases, make small technical customizations such as WordPress hooks, lightweight scripting, or small amounts of injected JavaScript

* participate in screen-share sessions with customers

* inspect settings, payloads, API behavior, and structured data to identify setup or integration issues

* over time, lead go-lives independently using well-understood technical playbooks, including cases that require light customization



Automation and Structured Operations



As the role grows, you will help identify and close the missing pieces required to automate chunks of workflow and make operations more efficient, less error-prone, and easier to diagnose.



You will:



* work with JSON, YAML, CSV, and other structured data used in our systems and processes

* use APIs, logs, internal tools, and technical artifacts to investigate issues and confirm behavior

* build or maintain light scripts, prompts, checklists, and internal artifacts that make the team more effective

* help codify recurring troubleshooting, QA, and go-live patterns so the organization gets stronger over time

* identify repetitive manual work, unclear handoffs, and avoidable sources of error, then help structure better ways of working

* contribute to a longer-term path into deeper technical operations, QA automation, or development-support work



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE IN THE FIRST 90 DAYS



* technical issues are reproduced more quickly and escalated more cleanly

* known issues are increasingly handled through clear playbooks rather than repeated ad hoc investigation

* QA catches issues earlier

* go-lives become smoother

* Development spends less time on preventable support interruptions

* leadership is pulled into emergency situations less often because problems are identified sooner and clients are kept informed earlier

* repetitive operational work starts getting documented, structured, or automated



REQUIREMENTS



* strong written and spoken English

* availability to work 8 hours per day within US hours, with US Central overlap required

* experience in technical support, Level 2+ support, QA, implementation, or a similar troubleshooting-heavy role

* familiarity with ecommerce systems (e.g. WordPress/WooCommerce, Shopify, and/or BigCommerce)

* strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills

* comfort working with JSON, YAML, CSV, and other structured technical information

* comfort reading API documentation and inspecting request/response behavior

* ability to write clear bug reports, technical notes, escalation summaries, and playbooks

* strong follow-through and self-direction

* reliable internet and a professional video-call environment

* familiarity using AI tools for drafting, analysis, or structured work, along with the judgment to verify correctness



PREFERRED



* experience using browser dev tools, Postman, curl, or similar debugging tools

* basic JavaScript, Python, PHP, or scripting ability

* experience validating integrations, webhooks, configuration, or data mappings

* experience with regression testing or QA process discipline



COMPENSATION



* starting at $900 to $1,200 USD per month depending on experience, hours, and demonstrated technical ability

* paid trial project before full offer

* long-term position



We are not trying to be the highest-paying listing for this type of role.



We are trying to be one of the best long-term opportunities for someone who wants to grow, take ownership, and become increasingly valuable to the team.



This role qualifies for our Growth and Incentive Program, where strong performance can lead to performance bonuses and permanent compensation increases as the company grows.



We also have a culture of continuous improvement. People who raise standards, build better playbooks, reduce repeat issues, improve workflows, increase automation, and make the company more effective can earn greater trust, responsibility, and upside over time.



We reward people who take ownership, raise standards, and deliver consistent results.



WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT



This is not a ticket-processing role.



You will not be judged only by how many tickets you touch.



You will be valued for:



* solving real problems

* improving how the company operates

* reducing repeated issues

* creating clarity

* building systems and playbooks that scale



For the right person, this is a real growth path — not just a support seat.



Over time, strong performance here can grow into:



* deeper QA ownership

* more responsibility for go-lives and technical implementations

* support workflow automation

* technical operations leadership

* selected development-adjacent work



HOW TO APPLY



We take hiring seriously because we are protective of our high-performing team and this is a long-term role. Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered.



It is okay to use AI to help organize or polish your application. In fact, we expect you to. But your answers must still reflect your own experience, judgment, and thinking.



To confirm that you read this posting carefully, start your application with this exact phrase:



I Raise the Bar.



Please include all of the following:



1. Availability

   Your exact US-hours availability, including your time zone and working hours.



2. Ownership story

   In 5 to 8 sentences, describe one real technical support, QA, or integration issue you owned end-to-end. Tell us what happened, what you did, how you approached it, and what the outcome was.



3. Technical triage response

   Respond to this scenario:



A customer reports that checkout verification is failing for most users on their live site, but the team cannot reproduce it in staging.



Please include:



* what you would check first

* the clarifying questions you would ask

* the likely categories of root cause you would consider

* what you would communicate back to the team or customer next



We read every serious application carefully.



If you take ownership, solve problems, communicate clearly, and want to grow, we would like to hear from you.

title: Appointment Setter – Music Industry (US Leads, Night Shift)
description:
Appointment Setter – Music Industry (US Leads, Night Shift)

— Iconent Group



US Music Management Company — Appointment Setter (Night Shift, Philippines)

We are a US-based artist management and distribution company partnered with major labels. We work exclusively with independent hip-hop/rap artists in the US market.

We are hiring ONE Appointment Setter to call warm inbound US leads, qualify them using a structured script, and book qualified artists for a strategy call with our Artist Development Manager.

This is NOT customer service. This is NOT cold calling. This is a high-performance sales qualification role with a strict script, scoring system, and daily KPIs. If you don't like following systems, this isn't for you.



The Role

You will be the first voice our leads hear. Your job is to:

* Call warm US leads who applied through our website or ads (inbound only — no cold calling)

* Follow a structured qualification script with a built-in scoring system (you don't improvise — you execute)

* Identify whether the lead has real budget, real commitment, and a real project — or is wasting time

* Book qualified leads for a 30-minute Zoom call with our Artist Development Manager

* Confirm appointments via call the morning of the closer call (show-rate is YOUR KPI)

* Update CRM tags, Google Sheets tracker, and call evaluations after every call

* Process call recordings through an AI tool to generate lead summaries

You are the filter. Bad leads that get through cost the company a wasted closer slot. Good leads that you miss cost the company revenue. Both are tracked.



Schedule

* Monday to Friday (Saturday optional based on volume)

* 01:00 AM – 09:00 AM Manila Time (12:00 PM – 8:00 PM US Eastern)

* Fixed night shift — non-negotiable

* You must be comfortable working these hours long-term — not "willing to try"



Requirements (non-negotiable)

English:

* Near-native or native-level spoken English

* Neutral accent — if US leads ask "where are you from?" more than once per shift, your accent is too strong

* You must sound confident, natural, and conversational on the phone — not scripted or robotic

* Written English must be clean (you'll write call evaluations and CRM notes daily)

Experience:

* Minimum 1 year of experience calling US-based leads (B2C or B2B)

* Experience with appointment setting, lead qualification, or inside sales — NOT customer support, NOT chat support, NOT
-only roles

* Must have experience following a structured call script with qualification criteria

Preferred (not required but strong advantage):

* Experience in the music industry, entertainment, artist management, or creative services

* Experience with high-ticket sales ($500-$5,000 range)

* Familiarity with CRM tools (FluentCRM, HubSpot, or similar)

* Familiarity with Google Sheets, Calendly, Zoom

Technical:

* Minimum 25 Mbps download / 10 Mbps upload (you will be required to show a speed test)

* Quiet, private workspace — no background noise, no shared rooms, no internet cafes

* Backup internet connection (mobile hotspot or secondary ISP)

* Own headset with noise cancellation

Personality:

* You follow systems. You don't "add your own touch" to a script without permission.

* You are comfortable hearing "no" 20 times a day and moving to the next call

* You can ask direct questions about someone's financial situation without getting awkward

* You don't take 45-minute calls when the script says 15 minutes

* You self-evaluate honestly after every call (written evaluation is mandatory, not optional)



What You Will NOT Do

* You will NOT pitch the service or explain pricing — that's the closer's job

* You will NOT give your personal opinion on the lead's music

* You will NOT have long "friendly" conversations — the call is 15 minutes, not 30

* You will NOT skip qualification steps because the lead "seems nice"

* You will NOT book leads who don't meet the criteria just to fill slots



Daily KPIs

* 80-100 call attempts per shift

* 15-25 leads reached

* 5-10 qualified

* 3-5 booked for closer call

* Show-rate on booked calls: target 70%+

* CRM + Tracker updated within 5 minutes of each call

* After-call evaluation completed for every reached lead



Compensation

Base: $300–$450/month (depending on experience and English level)

Bonuses (after 30-day probation):

* 3% of deal value on every closed-won lead you qualified and booked

* Monthly bonus for show-rate above 75%

Payment via Wise, PayPal, or direct bank transfer.



Tools You Will Use

* Zoiper — VoIP softphone for calling US numbers

* FluentCRM — lead database, tags, contact management

* Google Sheets — booked calls tracker (shared with closer)

* Calendly — booking closer call slots

* Claude AI — processing call recordings into lead summaries

* WhatsApp — team communication

* Discord — internal voice calls with closer

* Zoom — closer calls are on Zoom (you don't join, but you book them)

Training on all tools will be provided. You are expected to be operational within 5 business days.



How to Apply

Your application MUST include ALL of the following. Incomplete applications will be automatically rejected.

At the top of your message, write: "Night Shift – Ready"

Then include:

1. 2-3 sentences about yourself — not your life story. Who you are, where you're based, why this role.

2. Your calling experience — specifically: how many months/years calling US leads, what industry, what was your role (setter, closer, CS), what tools you used. If you've never called US leads, do not apply.

3. A 60-90 second voice recording answering this question: "An artist tells you he's been making music for 10 years and spent $5,000 on promotion but has nothing to show for it. He says he's ready to invest but needs to 'figure out his finances first.' How would you respond?" — This is not a trick question. We want to hear your English, your tone, and whether you can think on your feet. Record on Vocaroo, Google Drive, or any link we can open.

4. Internet speed test screenshot (use speedtest.net — must show download, upload, and ping)

5. Confirm your availability: "I confirm I can work 01:00 AM – 09:00 AM Manila Time, Monday to Saturday, long-term."

6. Answer this question in writing: "What is the difference between a setter and a closer? Why does the setter exist?"



Do NOT Apply If:

* Your English is intermediate or "conversational" — we need near-native

* You have only done chat support,
 support, or virtual assistant work

* You cannot commit to night shift long-term

* You don't have a quiet workspace

* You think "appointment setting" means reading a script like a robot



We are hiring ONE person. We will review voice recordings first, experience second. If your voice recording doesn't demonstrate clear, confident, natural English — your application stops there.

This is a long-term role with a growing company in the music industry. If you're the right fit, there is room to grow into a team lead position as we scale.

title: Video Editor at Markex Productions Remote | Part-Time | $5/hour
description:
**Job Description:**



****

*Remote | Part-Time | $5/hour*



Markex Productions is a digital audio education brand teaching beginners how to run sound with confidence. We create content that breaks down live sound, mixing, and audio engineering in a way that anyone can understand. Our audience is growing across YouTube, Instagram,
, and TikTok and we need someone who can keep up with that pace without being managed every step of the way.



This is a focused role. One job. Edit the content. Do it well. Do it consistently. If that sounds like you, keep reading.

---



**Responsibilities:**



**Video Editing**

You will receive raw footage through Google Drive and deliver polished, platform-ready content five days a week. That means short-form vertical content for Instagram,
, and TikTok ranging from 10 seconds to 2 minutes, and long-form horizontal content for YouTube ranging from 5 to 30 minutes. You handle the cuts, captions, pacing, transitions, and brand consistency. Every video that leaves your hands should look like it belongs to a professional brand because it does.



**Content Eye**

This is an audio education brand. The content is technical and the audience is learning something real. You do not need to be a sound engineer but you need to understand the space well enough to cut a video that makes sense. If you can watch a clip about EQ or gain structure and know what matters, you are already ahead of most applicants.

---



**Requirements:**



Proficient in video editing. CapCut, DaVinci Resolve, Adobe Premiere, or similar. You bring your own tools and you already know how to use them at a high level.



Beginner to intermediate knowledge of audio and sound. Terms like EQ, gain, live sound, and mixing should not be new to you. This is non-negotiable. You are editing educational content and context matters.



Fluent in English, written and spoken. You will be reading briefs, managing tasks in Notion, and communicating directly with the founder.



Reliable internet connection and access to Google Drive daily.



Detail-oriented and able to work independently without reminders.



If you have read this far, include the word "Soundcheck" in the subject line of your application.

---



**How to Apply:**

Send a 30 second and under video of why you would be a good fit for this role. Also include a screenshot of your internet speed. A video editing portfolio with both short-form and long-form samples is preferred but not required. Applicants who do not follow these instructions will not be considered. We look forward to hearing from you.

title: Remote Operations Manager / Transaction Coordinator — Miami Rental Real Agent Estate Team
description:
About us

We are a growing Miami-based real estate team specializing in tenant-side rental representation across Miami-Dade County. Our team includes a lead agent, a caller, and an operations VA. We are adding a key hire to take ownership of our daily operations, transaction management, and team coordination so our agent can stay focused on showings and client relationships.



About the role

This is not a basic VA position. You will be the number two in the business. You own the back end, transactions, team accountability, pipeline oversight, and daily operations. Over time this role grows into a full operations manager position as the team scales.



What you will own



Transaction coordination

— Collect and organize all tenant documents (ID, pay stubs, bank statements)

— Manage RentSpree application submissions and track status through to approval

— Communicate with landlords and property managers on application progress

— Upload and organize all files in GoHighLevel CRM



Team oversight

— Review daily EOD reports from caller and ops VA — flag anything needing agent attention

— Hold team accountable to daily tasks and reporting standards

— Compile a single daily briefing for the lead agent — key updates, flags, wins

— Escalate problems before they become deals lost



Pipeline and operations

— Monitor pipeline for gaps — no lead should go more than 24 hours without a touch

— Confirm tomorrow's showings are fully set up — calendar green, lockbox codes secured

— Draft and route offer
s and texts for agent approval before sending

— Manage outstanding admin, SOP updates, and vendor follow-ups



What we are looking for

— Experience in US real estate operations, transaction coordination, or property management

— Strong written English, clear communicator, no hand-holding needed

— Natural organizer who catches problems before they are problems

— Experience with GoHighLevel, Google Workspace, or similar platforms

— Someone who wants to grow into a real leadership role, not just complete tasks

— Available during US Eastern business hours, at least 9 AM to 5 PM overlap



Hours and compensation

title: *Travel Sales Specialist — Points & Award Booking*
description:
*What This Is*



Our client runs a high-end travel consultancy built entirely around the points economy. This is not a generalist travel role — it's a specialist position for someone who already lives in this world and wants to get paid to do it at scale.



The core insight driving this hire: real luxury travel today isn't bought with cash. It's engineered through the intelligent movement of points across US banking ecosystems into premium airline and hotel inventory. The candidate we're looking for already knows this — and already does it.



*The Role*



You'll serve as a high-level advisor to global clients, building travel solutions rather than just booking trips. That means identifying the right transfer windows, the right partner sweet spots, and the right booking paths to deliver value that clients couldn't find on their own. If you get genuine satisfaction from cracking a complex award itinerary that looked impossible on the surface, this is the job.



*What You'll Be Doing*



- Executing high-value award bookings that require real fluency in bank-to-airline/hotel transfer mechanics, availability patterns, and alliance routing rules

- Advising clients on how to deploy their points portfolios for maximum CPP — and knowing when to hold, when to transfer, and when a cash fare actually wins

- Staying current on the issuer landscape: devaluations, transfer bonuses, new card launches, and anything that shifts the calculus on Chase UR, Amex MR, Citi TYP, and their partners

- Handling the technical heavy lifting — married segments, phantom availability, manual reissues, the stuff that requires knowing where to look and how to push



*What You Need*



You know the difference between a fixed-value point and a transferable currency without having to think about it. You understand why a 1:1 transfer to one partner beats a 25% bonus to another in certain scenarios — and you can explain that to a client clearly. You've navigated booking engines across multiple alliances, you know which ones surface real availability and which ones don't, and you have a mental model of which programs are worth transferring into today versus six months ago.



You don't need a banking background, but you do need to understand how the major US issuers work, how their products are structured, and how they connect to the loyalty ecosystem.



Honestly — you're probably the person in your circle that everyone comes to when they want to book a trip with points. This is that, as a career.



*Logistics*



- Fully remote, open globally

- Commission-based compensation — structured to reward people who consistently deliver (details in interviews)

- Targeted search, not a mass posting



If this is already how you think about travel, let's talk.

title: Property Operations Lead - Remote
description:
Location: Remote 

Schedule: Monday–Friday, 8:00am–6:00pm Eastern Time (USA) 

Compensation: $6/hour + monthly performance bonus 



SteelSafe Self Storage runs remote, online-first storage: rent online, get instant access, move in today. That only works when units are clean, functional, and ready to rent fast. When turns stall, inventory dies. 



We’re hiring a Property Manager with one scoreboard: more rent-ready units, faster turns. 



What you get: 

A clear scoreboard and a role that matters. 

High trust and real autonomy. 

Direct access to leadership and fast decisions. 

A system-driven operation (we run on Asana). 

Performance bonus opportunity tied to ops execution. 



What you’ll do: 

Run fast turns: Unit move?outs to Ready?to?Rent (R2R). 

Manage boots on the ground(BOTG) contractors and vendors. 

Create and drive work orders to completion with proof (photos/video). 

Own site readiness: gates, locks, cameras, lighting, cleanliness, curb appeal. 

Handle property projects end?to?end (example: order a sign, coordinate install, confirm with proof).

Median Time?to?Rent?Ready (TTRR) after move?out is ? 4 days. 

Rent?Ready Unit % stays high and trending up. 

Work orders dispatch quickly and close with proof. 

BOTG tasks close on time with proof; reconciliation stays current. 



Weekly scorecard is posted and drives action. 

The authority and tools you’ll have: 

You can fix what’s broken: turn workflow, proof standards, vendor SLAs, BOTG execution, inspection cadence, and enforce it. 

You can approve vendor spend up to $50 per job. Above that requires approval before work begins. 

Asana as the operating system (tasks, checklists, proof). 



This role is a fit if you: 

Own outcomes and like a clear scoreboard. 

Move fast, follow through, and don’t close tasks without proof. 

You can manage vendors and contractors firmly and professionally. 

Think in checklists, photos, and deadlines. 

Care about security, cleanliness, and a great customer experience.

What You’ll Be Doing Every Day:

• Qualifying: Using the "4 Pillars" to screen sellers (Motivation, Property Condition, Timeline, and Asking Price).

What We’re Looking For:

Compensation & Perks

title: Senior Influencer Marketing Specialist
description:
About Us:



Bellvines is a YouTube Strategy company. We provide clients with the experience and expertise needed to succeed on the world largest social video platform. From advising brands on how to maintain a presence on YouTube that gets noticed, to managing select talent and running influencer campaigns - we are your resident YouTube agency.



Our team is small, remote-first, and obsessed with doing great work.



About the Opportunity, and You:



You’ll be joining us at a pivotal 
nt.



As the demand for YouTube-adjacent expertise climbs, we are looking for a talented professional who brings years of influencer marketing experience to the table, along with an interest in talent management and the broader creator ecosystem.



This role will begin with ownership of creator outreach where you will shape how we identify, engage, and nurture YouTube partners. You'll own sourcing and relationship-building in the short-term for branded campaigns, and over time will have opportunities to expand into deeper talent-management duties such as structuring long-term deals, identifying rising talent for management, guiding creators’ business strategy, and influencing our broader service offering.



Role/Responsibilities:



- Discover & Evaluate Creators - Hunt for YouTube channels that align with varied campaign briefs, vetting performance data, audience fit, and brand safety.

- Creator Outreach & Relationship Building - Craft compelling outreach, run personalized follow-ups, and maintain clear, timely communication that converts prospects into partners.

- Negotiation & Scheduling - Align on deliverables, fees, timelines, and creative parameters with creative partners.

- Data Hygiene & Reporting - Maintain healthy reporting standards, and surface insights as needed.

- Collaboration - Work closely with strategy and account leads to match creators with campaign goals and ensure post-campaign learnings feed back into future outreach.





What Makes You a Fit:



- 3 - 6 years running influencer or creator-marketing programs, with a strong background in YouTube.

- Proven ability to hit ambitious outreach targets and close influencer collaborations.

- Polished written voice and confidence negotiating directly with creators or their reps.

- Fluency in spreadsheets/CRMs

- Genuine enthusiasm for the creator economy



How to Apply:



- Candidates who are keen to stand out should
 a resume and a concise paragraph describing a YouTube campaign you were involved in to 
 with the subject line “Influencer Marketing Specialist – [Your Name]”.

title: AI OPERATIOR - Video Editing
description:
We produce faceless YouTube compilation videos across multiple channels. We are looking for a video editor who is comfortable using AI tools — specifically Claude and Hyperframes — to handle the bulk of the editing, then applies a final human polish to bring the output up to standard before delivery.



This is not a traditional editing role. The AI does most of the heavy lifting. Your job is to set it up correctly, iterate on its output, and make sure the final product is clean and consistent with our format.



The AI gets you speed. Your job is to make the final result publishable.



-



WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING



Before applying, you must watch this video so you understand the workflow:







This shows the AI-assisted editing process using Claude and Hyperframes that you will be expected to use.



Workflow summary:

 • Collect raw video clips, script, and assets

 • Use Claude (Claude Design or Claude Code) and Hyperframes to generate the first version of the video with motion graphics, captions, overlays, and animations

 • Iterate on the AI output by giving feedback and refining it

 • Do a final manual polish pass to fix issues and improve quality before delivery



--



WHAT I NEED FROM YOU

 • Comfortable using AI tools in your editing workflow

 • Willing to use Claude and Hyperframes as a core part of your process

 • Ability to follow a repeatable format and not deviate from it

 • Ability to execute feedback precisely, not loosely interpret it

 • Strong communication and responsiveness

 • Daily updates while a project is active

 • No disappearing mid-project



--



DEADLINES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)



Every project follows this structure:

 • 24-hour check-in after assignment

 • 48-hour progress update with visible work

 • 72-hour final delivery deadline



If any checkpoint is missed, the project is reassigned to someone else.



--



HOW THE HIRING PROCESS WORKS



Step 1 — Sample edit

You will receive a short clip package and brief. You will use the AI workflow to produce a 60-second sample edit.



Step 2 — Feedback round

I will give you specific written feedback. You will apply it in one revision.



Step 3 — Decision

If the revision meets the standard, we proceed to the full video and you are paid on delivery.

If it does not, the job is reassigned.



There is one revision on the sample. Decisions are made quickly.



--



PAYMENT

 • Paid per completed video

 • Payment released after final delivery

 • Consistent work available for reliable editors

 • Editors who deliver fast and consistently will be prioritized for ongoing work



--



TO APPLY



Start your message with the word READY so I know you read everything.



Then send:

 • Confirmation that you watched the required video

 • Whether you have used Claude Code, Claude Design, Hyperframes, or similar tools

 • 2–3 examples of your editing work (AI-assisted or traditional)

 • Your typical turnaround time for a 10–15 minute video



Applications without examples or without confirming the video will not be reviewed.

title: Recruitment Manager / Healthcare Recruiter (Remote)
description:
We are looking for a dedicated and experienced Recruitment Manager (Virtual Assistant) to join our team in supporting a growing Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) company. If you have a passion for healthcare recruitment and a proven track record of finding high-quality talent, we want to hear from you



The Role

In this position, you will lead the end-to-end recruitment lifecycle for vital healthcare roles, specifically Registered Behavior Technicians (RBTs) and Board Certified Behavior Analysts (BCBAs). Your goal is more than just filling seats; you will be responsible for ensuring successful hires, strong candidate retention, and long-term cultural fit



Key Responsibilities

Full-Cycle Management: Handle everything from job posting and sourcing to screening, interviewing, and onboarding support.

Strategic Sourcing: Utilize platforms like LinkedIn and Indeed to find qualified RBT and BCBA professionals.

Candidate Experience: Conduct initial screenings for qualifications and culture fit, maintaining consistent follow-ups and a positive experience

Coordination: Manage the scheduling of both virtual and in-person interviews.

Tracking & Reporting: Maintain organized candidate tracking via Excel or similar tools and work closely with the client to understand evolving hiring needs.

Retention Focus: Support and implement strategies to improve long-term candidate engagement and retention.



Requirements

Experience: Proven recruitment background, ideally within the healthcare or ABA industry.

Expertise: Strong understanding of hiring processes for RBTs and BCBAs.

Communication: Excellent verbal and written English skills.

Mindset: Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple roles simultaneously.

Work Style: Ability to work independently with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.



Nice to Have

Experience recruiting within the U.S. market (specifically Ohio).

Familiarity with ATS or CRM tools.

General knowledge of ABA practices or healthcare staffing.



Schedule

Type: Full-time.

Hours: Primarily aligned with U.S. Business Hours (9:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST).

Flexibility: Ability to respond to candidates outside of standard hours when necessary to ensure a smooth process



How to Apply (Non-Negotiable)

To be considered for this role, please ensure you submit the following:



Your Updated CV

An Intro Video (introducing yourself and your relevant experience)

Your Active WhatsApp Numbee

Please keep your lines open; we will be reaching out to qualified candidates via WhatsApp for the next steps.

title: Performance Account Manager and Creative Strategist | DTC Performance Marketing Agency (Remote, Full-Time)
description:
ABOUT THIS ROLE:

I am a performance marketing agency owner specializing in Meta advertising and Klaviyo
 marketing for DTC and fashion brands. I have been in e-commerce for six years and have built and scaled seven-figure brands. My current client roster includes recognized names in fashion, apparel, and accessories.



I am looking for a full-time Account Manager and Creative Strategist to work directly with me and grow long-term as we continue building out the team. This is not a short-term freelance project. I am looking for someone who wants to be here in a year and be a bigger part of what we are building.



WHAT THIS ROLE IS ACTUALLY ABOUT:

The most important part of this job is creative strategy. That means looking at what is working inside Meta ad accounts, identifying trends in performance data, and translating those insights into strong creative briefs for video editors, graphic designers, and brand teams so they can produce content that actually converts. If you do not understand why one ad outperforms another or you have never written a creative brief, this is not the right fit.



Beyond creative strategy, you will be managing the day-to-day of multiple client accounts. That includes keeping
 calendars on track in Klaviyo, coordinating with designers and vendors to hit deadlines, QA-ing campaigns before they go live, and helping with reporting and client communication. You will be expected to operate with high autonomy. I should not need to follow up with you.



WHAT YOU WILL OWN:

- Analyzing Meta ad account performance and identifying what creative angles, hooks, and formats are winning

- Writing detailed creative briefs for video editors, designers, and brand owners to produce ad content

- Managing the
 marketing calendar across multiple Klaviyo accounts and keeping sends on schedule

- QA-ing campaigns, flows, and ads before anything goes live

- Coordinating deliverables with offshore designers and AI-assisted creative teams

- Weekly performance reporting across Meta and Klaviyo

- Supporting client communication with clear, professional written updates

- Back-office coordination including invoicing and deliverable tracking



WHAT I AM LOOKING FOR:

You have real agency experience. You have sat inside Meta Ads Manager and understood what the numbers mean, not just pulled reports. You have written briefs that actual designers and video editors used to make real content. You are high-agency, meaning you figure things out, you move fast, and you do not wait to be told what to do. You want to grow and are genuinely excited by the idea of being part of building something, not just maintaining it.



You do not need to know everything on day one. I am willing to teach the right person. What I cannot teach is the drive to learn, the ability to think strategically, and the discipline to operate independently.



COMPENSATION:

This is a full-time role. Base pay is $1,500 to $2,800 per month depending on your experience and background. On top of that, there is a performance-based incentive structure tied to client results and account growth. The right candidate will not be capped at the base.



HOW TO APPLY:

To apply, send me a message that covers the following:

1 - The agencies you have worked for and what you were responsible for at each one. Be specific about what you actually did day to day.

2 - The Meta ad accounts you have managed. Tell me what type of brands they were, roughly what monthly spend you were working with, and what your role was in the account.

3 - The
 accounts or programs you have managed. Same thing -- what was the brand, what did you own, and what did you do there.

4 - A creative brief you have written, if you have one to share. If not, describe the process you use to brief a designer or video editor based on ad performance data.

5 - Your portfolio and resume.

6 - What genuinely excites you about this role.



That is it. I am not looking for a perfect application. I am looking for someone who can clearly communicate what they have done and show me they understand what this job actually requires.

title: HTML5 Banner Developer
