title: Commercial Real Estate Appraisal Analyst
description:
**IMPORTANT**

To apply for this role, only use this link:





Direct messages via onlinejobs.ph are not accepted.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------



Location: Remote (US hours)    



Type: Full-Time



Hours: 4:00 AM – 1:00 PM CST, Monday to Friday



Salary: PHP 80,000 – PHP 110,000 (PHP 100,000 – PHP 140,000 for MAI candidacy or proven US CRE track record)



 



About Pahroo



Pahroo Appraisal and Consultancy delivers commercial real estate valuations and advisory services across US markets, with a focus on assets underlying debt and equity investments. We work across office, industrial, multifamily, self-storage, and retail. The team is small, the work is rigorous, and the standards are high.



Working Hours — Please Read Before Applying

This role operates on US Central Standard Time: 4:00 AM – 1:00 PM CST, Monday to Friday. If you are based outside the US, please confirm this works for your circumstances before applying. It is a firm requirement.



The Role



You will sit within the valuation team, producing and supporting appraisal reports across a mixed commercial portfolio. The work is analytical, deadline-driven, and detail-critical. Once onboarded, you will be expected to work with a real degree of independence, drawing on sound US market knowledge to produce defensible value conclusions.



This is not an entry-level position. We need someone who understands why a number is what it is, not just how to arrive at it.



What You Will Do



 - Conduct research using industry databases — CoStar, REIS, RCA, PwC Real Estate Investor Survey, and broker market reports — to gather and interrogate comparable market evidence

 - Analyse property-level financial data including income statements, rent rolls, reimbursement schedules, and occupancy statistics to develop well-supported value indicators

 - Prepare collateral monitoring reports for assets underlying debt and equity investments across office, industrial, multifamily, self-storage, retail, and other property types

 - Produce USPAP-compliant appraisal reports ready for lender, investor, or regulatory review

 - Identify data gaps and market anomalies; flag assumptions that materially affect value conclusions

 - Manage competing report deadlines accurately without compromising on quality

 



What We Need



Required



 - Minimum 5 years of commercial real estate appraisal or valuation experience

 - Demonstrated, hands-on experience in the US property market — no learning curve on market fundamentals

 - Proficiency in ANOW, Total, and Excel-based valuation modelling

 - Working knowledge of USPAP requirements and their practical application in report preparation

 - Bachelor’s degree in Real Estate, Finance, Economics, or a closely related field

 - Strong written English — reports require clear, accurate, and defensible reasoning

 



Advantageous



 - MAI designation (Appraisal Institute) or active candidacy

 - RICS membership — relevant for internationally-based candidates with demonstrable US market exposure

 - Familiarity with CoStar, REIS, or RCA analytics platforms

 - Experience across two or more distinct asset classes

 



What We Offer



 - Base salary PHP 80,000 – PHP 110,000 per month

 - PHP 100,000 – PHP 140,000 for candidates with MAI candidacy or a demonstrable US CRE track record across multiple asset classes — we pay up for the right experience

 - Fully remote position

 - Clear onboarding with defined expectations from day one



**IMPORTANT**

To apply for this role, only use this link: 





Direct messages via onlinejobs.ph are not accepted.

title: Clinical Systems Administrator exp in CTMS
description:
The Clinical Systems Administrator is a full WFH role and will be the primary point of contact to provide day-to-day technical

and administrative support for various Clinical Systems, including the Clinical Trial Management System

(CTMS). This position includes system implementation, troubleshooting, user support, and optimizing

clinical systems for clinical research studies. The role will also help FHI Clinical use automation to

promote efficiency and effectiveness in our systems and processes.

Essential Functions:

• Handles day-to-day operations and system administration for a small portfolio of clinical

applications.

• Supports current users of the applications and may include provisioning new users.

• Troubleshoots user access issues.

• Assists projects for clinical applications as needed including but not limited to creation of

Accounts/Contacts within systems, entering subjects and subject visit dates.

• Assists with the planning and execution of interoperability activities of two or more systems.

• Collaborates with key stakeholders, to develop and maintain manuals, quick reference guides,

training materials, templates procedures, and other supportive procedural documents associated

with Clinical managed systems.

• Manages (including tracking) issues noted by system users, including those requiring submission

to Clinical system vendor helpdesk.

• Regarding the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS)

o Enters data into CTMS for specific projects, accounts, and contacts.

o Optimizes CTMS to function efficiently and effectively within the organization.

o Resolves helpdesk tickets specific to CTMS issues and questions including but not limited to:

visits completed in error with payments issued against them; assignment of staff to roles

and rights as needed; responding as necessary with re-training opportunities.

o Facilitates the implementation of the CTMS within the organization.

o Assists with managing access and roles within the CTMS system.

o Provides training to CTMS users as and when required

o Works with the individual Clinical Project Managers (CPM) and their delegates in the

management of each study CTMS.

o Works with Clinical Operations and Quality in generating metrics for review in CTMS

activities.

o Provides support, as necessary, with interface capabilities between the CTMS and eTMF

system for the transfer of study-specific documents to the study-specific eTMF.

o Maintains a thorough knowledge of the organization and adheres to all organizational

standards.

o Keeps immediate supervisor well-informed of activities and recommends corrective actions.

• Supports with maintaining all projects Smartsheet and updating the Smartsheet dashboards.

• Collaborates with identified stakeholders to assist in the development of organizational needs to

develop processes within CTMS for better workflow.

• Identifies organizational needs, addresses any problems, and develops processes within CTMS.

Collaborates with related departments on organizational processes perform using CTMS.

• Collaborates with vendor and FHIC’s stakeholders in CTMS new release management including

testing of new features; set up of new features; development of processes related to new features;

implementation and rollout; and processing and resolution of any identified issues.

• Assists in tracking of any identified bugs, defects, or enhancement CTMS requests from within

organization.

• Respects confidentiality in discussing consumer/participant, staff, volunteer, and organizational

matters; also maintains confidentiality of organization, project, fiscal and personnel related

information.

• May perform other duties not specifically listed in this job description as assigned by the manager.

Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:

• Knowledge of clinical research processes, including working knowledge of all functional areas of

clinical trial management (experience with relational databases is helpful).

• Strong communication skills (verbal and written) to express complex ideas.

• Exceptional attention to detail and follow-through.

• Excellent organizational and interpersonal skills.

• Positive attitude and ability to interact with all levels of staff.

• Must be able to do multiple tasks and have a level of flexibility that can support group priorities across

the company and staff needs.

• Ability to train clinical staff at all levels within the company.

• Proficiency in working with Word, Excel, and PowerPoint as well as PDF software/programs (Adobe,

Foxit, etc.).

• Working knowledge of current ICH/GCP guidelines and applicable country specific regulations.

• Willingness and ability to travel domestically and internationally as required.

• Willingness and ability to work across multiple time zones.



If you qualify and are interested in the job, please send your updated CV to 
 and we will get in touch with you for a quick interview.

title: Customer Experience Virtual Assistant (Phone Based)
description:

 the company directly. Any candidate who contacts the company outside of the OnlineJobs system will be disqualified from consideration.



Customer Experience Virtual Assistant (Phone-Based, Process-Driven)



Job Overview



Are you someone who takes pride in handling customer interactions the right way—calmly, professionally, and consistently? Do you do well with clear expectations, scripts, and processes, and enjoy improving quickly through feedback? If so, Maid In America Cleaning Services, based in Clarksville, TN, wants to hear from you.



We’re seeking a Customer Experience Virtual Assistant to serve as a frontline point of contact for our customers and a dependable executor of structured processes. Working closely with our sales and customer experience teams, you’ll be responsible for answering inbound calls, handling customer requests, following defined scripts, and routing issues accurately.



This role is process-driven and phone-heavy, especially during training. The work can be fast-paced at times, but it is highly structured. Your ability to stay calm, follow instructions precisely, and take coaching seriously will have a direct impact on customer satisfaction and team efficiency.



What You’ll Do



- Answer & Handle Inbound Customer Calls: Be the first point of contact for customers and new inquiries, answering calls promptly, professionally, and calmly while following provided phone scripts.

- Follow Scripts & Decision Trees: Handle common customer needs—such as scheduling questions, reschedules, basic service inquiries, and initial issue intake—by following clear scripts and escalation rules exactly.

- Route Requests Accurately: Determine when a request can be handled directly and when it should be escalated to the appropriate tea
mber, ensuring clean handoffs and no dropped balls.

- Create a Positive Customer Experience: Make customers feel heard, cared for, and confident that their request is being handled, even when issues or complaints arise.

- Document Interactions & Updates: Log accurate notes and updates in our systems so the internal team always has clear context and next steps.

- Support Structured Administrative Workflows: Execute defined backend processes (task tracking, updates, file movement, and follow-through) by following SOPs and checklists without skipping steps.

- Receive Coaching & Improve Quickly: Actively apply feedback on calls, scripts, and workflows, improving speed, accuracy, and confidence over time.



Who We’re Looking For



- Process-Driven & Detail-Oriented: You’re comfortable following scripts, checklists, and SOPs exactly, and you don’t skip steps or “freestyle” your work.

- Calm & Professional on the Phone: You communicate clearly and confidently, stay composed with customers, and can handle questions or mild frustration without getting flustered.

- Highly Coachable: You take feedback seriously, apply it quickly, and improve fast—especially when it comes to phone scripts and customer handling.

- Reliable & Consistent: You show up on time, follow through on tasks, and can be counted on to do things the right way every time.

- Comfortable with Structure: You prefer clear expectations, defined processes, and knowing exactly what “good” looks like in your role.

- Tech-Comfortable: You’re comfortable working inside tools like Slack, shared documents, and task systems—or you can learn them quickly with instructions.

- Strong Communicator: Whether by phone, chat, or written notes, you communicate clearly, professionally, and respectfully.



Our Core Values



- Communication

- Connection

- Quality

- Service

- Teamwork



If you share these values, we want you on our team to help drive our company forward.



Important Note: Screening Step



SERIOUS APPLICANTS ONLY:

Complete our personality test to see if there’s a mutual fit:


/p/fzxN5FC9302s 

Give honest answers; there are no right or wrong results.



Submit your application with the subject line beginning with the core value we’ve listed that you most resonate with.



IMPORTANT: When applying, please use AT LEAST 5 Apply Points



Skills & Requirements

- Fluent English: Strong spoken and written English is required. This is a phone-based role, and customers must be able to understand you clearly and comfortably.

- Customer Service Experience: Prior experience handling customer inquiries or support requests over the phone is required.

- Process & Administrative Competence: You’re comfortable following multi-step processes, documenting information accurately, and keeping systems up to date.

- Attention to Detail: You consistently capture the right information, follow instructions precisely, and avoid careless errors.

- Coachability: You’re open to feedback, willing to be corrected, and able to apply coaching quickly—especially when learning phone scripts and customer-handling expectations.

- Technical Readiness:

  - Reliable computer with backup power options

  - Quality headset and microphone

  - Quiet workspace suitable for phone calls

- Ability to Work Monday-Friday, 8AM-5PM CST.

- Reliable Internet & Backup Options: Stable internet connection with a backup plan to ensure consistent availability and responsiveness.



Why Join Maid In America Cleaning Services?



- Impactful Role: Your quick execution and initiative will directly affect customer satisfaction and operational efficiency.

- Growth-Oriented Culture: We embrace new ideas, reward proactive thinking, and train daily for continual improvement.

- Supportive Team: Collaborate with a group that values each other’s contributions and thrives on a feedback-rich environment.



If you’re passionate about productivity, eager to work in a fast-paced setting, and confident in your ability to deliver high-quality results quickly, apply now and become an integral part of the Maid In America family!



Apply Today: Submit your application per the instructions above. We look forward to learning more about you!

title: CPA /Senior Accountant
description:
Are you ready to take your accounting career to the next level? We are a US-based real estate investment and management company seeking a talented and motivated Senior Accountant to join our growing international team. This is a remote, full-time role based in the Philippines, where you’ll work directly with our US leadership during US business hours.

If you’re passionate about real estate accounting, thrive in fast-paced environments, and want to contribute to exciting projects across multiple entities, this is the opportunity for you.



What You’ll Do

As our Senior Accountant, you’ll be a key player in keeping our financial operations accurate, compliant, and efficient. You won’t just be crunching numbers—you’ll be driving clarity and insights that help us grow.

Manage the full accounting cycle, including journal entries, reconciliations, and month/quarter-end close.

Prepare accurate financial statements and management reports.

Handle accounts payable/receivable, tenant ledgers, and vendor payments

Oversee bank reconciliations and investigate discrepancies.

Assist with budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis.

Partner with CPAs during audits and tax filings.

Ensure compliance with US GAAP, Cash, and Modified Cash standards.

Maintain accurate financial data in AppFolio and QuickBooks Online (QBO).

Provide insightful reporting and analysis to the CFO and leadership team.



What We’re Looking For

We want someone who is not only technically strong but also proactive, communicative, and eager to add value.



Required:

Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field.

3–5 years of accounting experience with a US-based real estate company.

Strong knowledge of property management and hospitality accounting.

Hands-on experience with AppFolio and QuickBooks Online (QBO).

Excellent English communication skills, both written and spoken.

Meticulous attention to detail, organizational strength, and reliability in meeting deadlines.

Flexibility to work US business hours.



Preferred:

CPA license (Philippines or US).

Experience with multi-entity structures and consolidations.

Familiarity with investor reporting and distributions.

Strong Excel skills, especially for financial modeling and analysis.



Why You’ll Love Working With Us

Competitive pay with long-term career growth opportunities.

A chance to work directly with US executives and decision-makers.

Exposure to exciting real estate and hospitality projects across the US.

A professional yet supportive team culture where your work is valued.

A stable role in an expanding international company with room to grow.



Here, your skills will make an immediate impact, and your career will grow alongside our company’s success.



If you’re ready to take ownership, make a difference, and build a rewarding career in real estate accounting, we’d love to meet you.



Apply now and become an essential part of our accounting team.

title: Matching Specialist
description:
Apex Social brings together experienced educators, therapists and care providers with families who have special care needs and provides personal & career development through a unique, collaborative work experience for Care Professionals in a new state or new country.



Our Mission

-Unlocking children’s greatest potential through integrating skilled Care Professionals into their family’s daily life.

-Empowering Children to develop to their fullest abilities.

-Empowering Care Professionals by providing personal & career development, experiential learning and cultural enrichment through a unique, collaborative work experience.

-Empowering Families by providing specialized, skilled child care and cultural exchange in the home through an individualized recruiting and matching process.

-Empowering Schools & Universities by providing career coaching and support for their students and alumni to gain interdisciplinary paid work experience & continuing education in Germany, the United States and Australia.

-Empowering Tea
mbers by providing a collaborative, remote work environment with opportunities for training and growth as well as a commitment to our mission ensuring that we find purpose, fulfillment and a sense of belonging in our careers.



Role Summary: 

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented Matching Specialist to join our dynamic team. The Matching Specialist will play a crucial role in connecting individuals with the most suitable opportunities, programs, or services offered by APEX Social Group. This role requires exceptional interpersonal skills, a keen understanding of individual needs, and a strong ability to identify and cultivate relevant connections.



Responsibilities:

-Collaborate with Manager and leadership to understand role requirements and priorities.

-Source, screen, and shortlist high-quality candidates efficiently.

-Manage multiple hiring pipelines independently.

-Ensure fast, accurate matching of candidates post-onboarding.

-Maintain candidate engagement and provide updates to stakeholders.

-Recommend process improvements to increase time-to-hire and candidate quality.



Qualifications & Experience:

-Minimum 10 years in HR, recruitment, or talent acquisition.

-Proven full-cycle recruiting experience, ideally in US-based or international contexts.

-Strong judgment in evaluating candidates and making recommendations.

-Experience with agency recruiting or high-volume hiring is a plus.

-Excellent communication, organizational, and stakeholder management skills.

-Able to work independently and hit the ground running after onboarding.



Working Hours: PST or EST Timezone. Occasional flexibility may be required to accommodate urgent scheduling needs or communication with U.S.-based participants.

title: Accountant / Bookkeeper / Finance Executive
description:
Job Overview

We are seeking a detail-oriented and reliable finance professional to manage day-to-day accounting and financial operations. The ideal candidate will ensure accurate financial reporting, maintain proper records, and support overall business financial health.



Key Responsibilities

Handle full set of accounts (AP, AR, GL)

Maintain accurate bookkeeping and financial records

Prepare monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports

Perform bank reconciliations and ensure timely closing

Process invoices, payments, and expense claims

Manage accounts payable and receivable functions

Assist in budgeting and forecasting

Ensure compliance with accounting standards and regulations

Liaise with auditors, tax agents, and relevant authorities

Support payroll processing and statutory submissions (e.g. CPF, GST if applicable)

Improve financial processes and internal controls



Requirements

Diploma/Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field

Minimum 1–3 years of relevant experience (adjust based on seniority)

Knowledge of accounting software (e.g. Xero, QuickBooks, SAP)

Strong attention to detail and accuracy

Good organizational and time management skills

Proficient in Microsoft Excel

Ability to work independently and meet deadlines



Preferred Qualifications

Experience handling full sets of accounts

Familiarity with local tax and compliance requirements

Prior experience in SMEs or fast-paced environments

Key Traits

Meticulous and responsible

Trustworthy with high integrity

Proactive and solution-oriented

title: Shopify Store Specialist
description:
HOW TO APPLY

To apply, please fill out the application form: 
/appzq4iqeIREQRVyo/pagwn3KJ9biX7VeNO/form



Given the high volume of applicants, we will not be able to consider applications not submitted through this form. Thank you for your understanding!



POSITION DETAILS: SHOPIFY STORE SPECIALIST



MISSION:

To drive E-commerce sales growth through proactive E-commerce account management, including timely and accurate listing management and optimization, digital storefront optimization, and marketing campaign ideation and execution, with the ultimate goal of creating a thriving and sustainable online business that meets the evolving needs of our diverse customer base.



KEY OUTCOMES:

* Product Setup: Efficiently set up new products on assigned accounts according to agreed-upon listing standards, ensuring a 99.8% upload rate.



* Store Optimization: Within the first 6 months, boost our store's conversion rate to the industry average of 2.0%



* Record Maintenance: Maintain 100?curacy in Airtable record-keeping and weekly KPI report tracking.



* Inventory Feeds: Achieve 99?curacy in inventory feed uploads and maintain timely inventory updates, with no more than one error per quarter; do periodic spot checks of >10 items on each platform per week to verify site accuracy



* MAPP Adherence: Maintain 100% compliance with the company's MAPP; violations resolved within 72 hours



* New Ideas: Implement >2 major new ideas per quarter to improve account performance through a mix of competitor research and the latest Shopify/eCom sales strategies



OTHER REGULAR RESPONSIBILITIES:

* SOP Creation and Maintenance: Update/maintain SOPs that encompass the product setup process, store optimization, and more.



* Performance Analysis and Reporting: Every week, analyze sales and other KPIs to understand site performance, identify areas for improvement, and inform data-driven decision-making. Investigate any unexplained fluctuations and collaborate with customers to resolve issues when necessary.



* Cross-functional Collaboration: Monitor daily order flow and collaborate with internal teams such as Customer Service and Marketing to resolve issues and ensure a seamless and integrated approach to store management.



* Market Research: Keep tabs on competitor strategies to inform listing strategy and overall account strategy.



* Promotions and Discounts: Develop and manage promotional campaigns to drive sales, increase customer retention, and enhance brand visibility.



JOB REQUIREMENTS:

* Organized home office setup, free of noise and distractions, with a dual monitor setup strongly preferred.



* Reliable power and internet connections, with backup systems in place.



* Full-time position, 40 hrs/week minimum, with partial overlapping hours or entirely overlapping hours with US Central time



* 2-3 years of experience with Shopify site maintenance



* 1+ years of experience with Shopify site optimization, including CRO work



* Must have substantial previous Excel experience, including experience working with flat files



* Excellent English skills



* High attention to detail, with the ability to meticulously proofread for spelling and grammar



* Ability and willingness to independently learn new tools, products, or technology and make recommendations on trends to support the business



* Self-motivated, detail-oriented, and professional with the ability to multitask and prioritize workload; ability to work independently with minimal supervision is extremely important



* Ability to work with our team via
, Slack, and
/Zoom. Highly preferred, but not required: experience with Airtable and Notion



* Stable, trustworthy, highly dependable. Ideally, someone seeking a long-term relationship where we can both grow from the relationship



* Commitment to HiEnd Accents' mission and values: 
/3Unr0ub



About HiEnd Accents

HiEnd Accents designs and sells premium bedding and home textiles — quilts, sheets, decorative pillows, duvets, and more — that bring everyday luxury into people's homes. We've been family-owned and operated since 2005, now in our second generation of leadership, and are headquartered in Dallas, Texas.



We serve thousands of retailers, interior designers, department stores, and boutique hotels across the United States through four distinct brands spanning a range of styles and price points. Our growth has been recognized nationally: we've been named to the Inc. 5000 (a prestigious list of America's fastest-growing private companies) two years running, and have received multiple industry awards for product excellence.



We're a company of around 45 people — small enough that every person matters, established enough that you're joining something real and growing.



Discover more at 
.



Why HiEnd Accents?

We're growing our team in the Philippines because we believe in finding the best people regardless of geography — and investing in them for the long term.



As a family-owned company, we don't treat our tea
mbers as interchangeable. We take the time to build real working relationships, we care about your professional development, and we want every person who works with us to leave with stronger skills than when they started. That's a genuine commitment from our leadership, not a line in a job posting.



Learn more about our Philippines team here: Meet the HiEnd Accents Team in the Philippines (
) 



What you can expect:

You'll work directly with our core team in Texas — not through layers of management or a BPO intermediary. Your contributions will be visible, your ideas heard, and your growth supported.



-Compensation & Benefits— Competitive pay benchmarked to PH market rates for skilled roles, not entry-level floors. Paid time off, including vacation days and holidays, plus 13th-month pay.

- Real Career Growth— We're expanding quickly, which means new roles and responsibilities open up regularly. We actively develop our people through mentorship, skill-building, and increasing ownership of projects.

-A Team That Feels Like a Team — We're a close, collaborative group that celebrates wins together. You'll know your teammates by name — including leadership.

title: Hotel Account Manager (Remote)
description:
Rockenue is a tech-powered commercial management company for hotels across Europe. We handle revenue management, distribution, and tech implementation for a growing portfolio of properties.



We're hiring someone to manage a portfolio of hotel accounts end-to-end. You'll be the main point of contact for property partners — producing reports, flagging issues, and keeping everything running smoothly. 

You'll work inside OTAs like 
 and Expedia, channel managers, and hotel tech systems daily.



No hospitality experience needed. We'll train the right person. What we're looking for is harder to teach.



You should be:



Someone who picks up new software fast and genuinely enjoys it — complex dashboards and unfamiliar platforms excite you, not intimidate you. Already using AI tools like ChatGPT or Claude as a regular part of how you work. A take-ownership type with a strong work ethic — when something needs doing, you do it properly and fast without being asked. The kind of person people naturally come to because you're reliable and on top of things. A clear, professional communicator in English (written and verbal) who can keep clients confident and informed.



To apply: Send a short video intro (2–3 min) covering who you are, why Rockenue, and a quick example of how you use AI or automation in your work. Even a simple phone recording is fine — we care about energy, not production quality.



Competitive monthly salary (GBP/USD). Long-term position with real room to grow as the portfolio scales.

title: Process Support Executive
description:
PLEASE READ BEFORE APPLYING



We’re looking for a reliable, detail-focused Product Support Executive with experience supporting property or facilities-related businesses (e.g., property management, real estate operations, facilities management, tenant services, or building management).



You’ll work closely with our office managers, handling day-to-day admin and documentation so they can focus on client support and growth.



What You’ll Be Doing:

* Prepare and send invoices for services and products

* Process registrations, documentation, and related records

* Complete and submit required forms and paperwork

* Assist with permits, applications, and administrative documentation as needed

* Create simple, professional social media posts

* Organize digital files and maintain accurate records

* Provide general administrative support to managers and tea
mbers as needed

* Helping with customer-related follow-ups when needed



What We're Looking For:

* Strong English communication skills (written and verbal)

* Solid experience as a Virtual Assistant or in an administrative support role

* Background in supporting facilities or property service businesses such as Property Management, Real Estate Operations, Facilities Management, Tenant Services, or Building Management 

* Experience handling rebates, permits, or compliance paperwork 

* Comfortable creating basic social media content

* Tech-savvy and confident, learning new systems quickly

How to Apply :

Applications missing any of the above will not be reviewed.

title: Senior Google Ads Specialist (High-Budget Performance)
description:
We are seeking a high-level Google Ads Specialist who has real experience managing large monthly budgets and consistently delivering profitable results.

 What You’ll Do

 Requirements

 Applications without real data or proof will NOT be reviewed.



 How to Apply



Send your CV + required details to:





Subject:

[Google Ads Specialist] – Your Full Name

title: SA Operations Lead + Project Coordination (UK Hours)
description:
We are a UK-based property investment and serviced accommodation (SA) management company. We operate seven serviced accommodation units and focus on delivering a consistent, high-standard guest experience through strong operational systems and quality control. Alongside SA operations, we run two to three active refurbishment/development projects at any given time, coordinating contractors, procurement, documentation, and stakeholder communication to deliver projects efficiently and to a high standard.



This is a hands-on Operations Lead role responsible for execution across our serviced accommodation portfolio and coordination support across refurbishment/development projects. Your core focus is proactive quality control and consistent delivery: you’ll enforce standards through checklists and evidence, ensure guests have a smooth end-to-end experience, and reduce recurring issues by continuously improving SOPs. In parallel, you’ll support project coordination for refurbishments by running follow-ups with contractors and suppliers, managing documentation, and scheduling appointments to keep workstreams moving without drift.



We’re hiring an Operations Lead who can run a cadence, enforce quality standards, and coordinate delivery across both serviced accommodation and active refurb projects.



Monday- Sunday:10:00-18:00 ( with 1 hour break) UK time, Saturday or Sunday off for one day.

Salary :700 USD Per month+ KPI bonus



Core responsibilities

Serviced Accommodation operations (delivery + standards)

- Enforce unit standards using checklists, photo evidence, and “definition of done” quality bars.

- Manage guest communications: fast responses, professional tone, calm resolution, and proactive updates.

- Maintain an issues/defects log and ensure issues are fully closed with root-cause prevention (not just “ticket closed”).

- Coordinate cleaners and operational VAs: confirm turnovers, verify quality, and drive consistent performance.

- Monitor pricing and availability: check pricing rules are being applied, flag risks/opportunities, and escalate recommendations.

- Create and update listings: listing setup, copy accuracy, amenities, house rules, photo requirements, and platform compliance (
 as applicable).

- Maintain and improve SOPs: update processes based on recurring issues, guest feedback, and operational learnings.

Property project coordination (admin execution)

- Maintain a project tracker for tasks, owners, due dates, and blockers.

- Source and compare quotes: produce a quote comparison table and recommendation.

- Follow-up with contractors, suppliers, utilities, council, other professional, keep reference numbers, paper trail, and next-step ownership.

- Documentation: build/format/update project packs, submissions, and version control.

- Appointment scheduling: inspections, contractor visits, deliveries, surveys, and access coordination.

Reporting directly to company CEO



Tools and working style

- We use Lark for communication, tracking, and internal workflows.

- Google Drive for document storage and document control.

- PMS/pricing tools as applicable (Hostaway/PriceLabs or similar).



Must-have experience

- Evidence-based quality control: Create and used checklists/audits/photo evidence used to enforce standards and reduce recurring issues.

- Serviced accommodation or hospitality ops: guest comms + turnover coordination + issue resolution.

- Property management working experience

- Vendor/contractor coordination: proactive follow-up, scheduling, and timeline control.

- Strong written English: crisp guest messaging and concise leadership updates.

- SOP ownership: has maintained/improved SOPs based on operational reality, not just followed them.

Nice-to-have experience

title: Property Ops Lead – Guest Experience + Refurb Coordination
description:
We are a UK-based property investment and serviced accommodation (SA) management company. We operate seven serviced accommodation units and focus on delivering a consistent, high-standard guest experience through strong operational systems and quality control. Alongside SA operations, we run two to three active refurbishment/development projects at any given time, coordinating contractors, procurement, documentation, and stakeholder communication to deliver projects efficiently and to a high standard.



This is a hands-on Operations Lead role responsible for execution across our serviced accommodation portfolio and coordination support across refurbishment/development projects. Your core focus is proactive quality control and consistent delivery: you’ll enforce standards through checklists and evidence, ensure guests have a smooth end-to-end experience, and reduce recurring issues by continuously improving SOPs. In parallel, you’ll support project coordination for refurbishments by running follow-ups with contractors and suppliers, managing documentation, and scheduling appointments to keep workstreams moving without drift.



We’re hiring an Operations Lead who can run a cadence, enforce quality standards, and coordinate delivery across both serviced accommodation and active refurb projects.



Monday- Sunday:10:00-18:00 ( with 1 hour break) UK time, Saturday or Sunday off for one day.

Salary :700 USD Per month+ KPI bonus



Core responsibilities

Serviced Accommodation operations (delivery + standards)

- Enforce unit standards using checklists, photo evidence, and “definition of done” quality bars.

- Manage guest communications: fast responses, professional tone, calm resolution, and proactive updates.

- Maintain an issues/defects log and ensure issues are fully closed with root-cause prevention (not just “ticket closed”).

- Coordinate cleaners and operational VAs: confirm turnovers, verify quality, and drive consistent performance.

- Monitor pricing and availability: check pricing rules are being applied, flag risks/opportunities, and escalate recommendations.

- Create and update listings: listing setup, copy accuracy, amenities, house rules, photo requirements, and platform compliance (
 as applicable).

- Maintain and improve SOPs: update processes based on recurring issues, guest feedback, and operational learnings.

Property project coordination (admin execution)

- Maintain a project tracker for tasks, owners, due dates, and blockers.

- Source and compare quotes: produce a quote comparison table and recommendation.

- Follow-up with contractors, suppliers, utilities, council, other professional, keep reference numbers, paper trail, and next-step ownership.

- Documentation: build/format/update project packs, submissions, and version control.

- Appointment scheduling: inspections, contractor visits, deliveries, surveys, and access coordination.

Reporting directly to company CEO



Tools and working style

- We use Lark for communication, tracking, and internal workflows.

- Google Drive for document storage and document control.

- PMS/pricing tools as applicable (Hostaway/PriceLabs or similar).



Must-have experience

- Evidence-based quality control: Create and used checklists/audits/photo evidence used to enforce standards and reduce recurring issues.

- Serviced accommodation or hospitality ops: guest comms + turnover coordination + issue resolution.

- Property management working experience

- Vendor/contractor coordination: proactive follow-up, scheduling, and timeline control.

- Strong written English: crisp guest messaging and concise leadership updates.

- SOP ownership: has maintained/improved SOPs based on operational reality, not just followed them.

Nice-to-have experience

- Listing creation and optimization (
).

- Pricing/availability monitoring and familiarity with revenue management tools (e.g., PriceLabs).

- Property management operations or refurb coordination admin.

Personality traits we’re looking for

- Conscientious and detail-accurate.

- Proactive follow-up and ownership mindset.

- Structured and process-driven (lives in checklists/trackers).

- Calm, professional, and solution-oriented with guests and vendors.

- Improves systems continuously rather than firefighting repeatedly.

- Well organised with multiple task and able to prioritise

title: Sales Associate Manager (Analyst) – Ticketing
description:
TO BE CONSIDERED, PLEASE ATTACH YOUR CV AND DESCRIBE YOUR EXPERIENCE IN TICKETING



Company Description



Our company specializes in developing innovative solutions within the live entertainment industry, focusing on enhancing fan experiences and engagement. By creating B2B and B2C systems, the company aims to provide tools and services that empower promoters, marketplaces, artists, and marketers to better serve their audiences. The company also explores cross-border inventory and content distribution opportunities while pioneering e-commerce concepts for underserved fans. Driven by innovation, the company is passionate about advancing the live entertainment ecosystem.

This roles sits within the Ticket Distribution/ Broker business.



Role Description



We are hiring a Sales Associate Manager to support the Sales Manager at a fast-growing ticketing business focused on distributing concerts, sports and other major live events across the UK, Europe and Asia.



This is a junior commercial role within the Sales team. The person will support on inventory acquisition, category management, and event analysis, while developing into a future Sales Manager with stronger ownership across pricing, category expertise, and new event identification.



We are looking for someone who is smart, highly analytical, detail-oriented, and eager to learn quickly. You must already have at least 1 year of experience in ticketing





Main responsibilities



Acquire inventory and/or coordinate inventory acquisition across key events

Support ticket category management

Help build ownership over one category, vertical or smaller market

Research events, supply/demand patterns, pricing and market behaviour

Support commercial analysis and business cases

Work closely with the Sales Manager on execution and opportunity sizing

Maintain trackers and keep commercial activity organised





Requirements



At least 1 year of experience in secondary market ticketing

Good understanding of inventory acquisition, management and pricing

Strong analytical mindset

Very comfortable with Excel/ Data visualisation tools

Strong attention to detail and reliability

Fast learner with good commercial judgment

Experience with Viagogo, StubHub, Skybox or similar tools is a strong plus

Background in Mathematics or another quantitative field is a big plus





This is a great role for someone who wants to build a long-term commercial career in ticketing and live entertainment, and grow into a broader Sales Manager role over time.



What we offer



Competitive package with a significant variable component tied to commercial results (2x OTE).

Opportunity to work and get mentored by the Directors and be a core part of the company’s hyper-growth story having the support form the rest of the team.

Remote-first setup with flexibility, while following Europe (GMT) working hours.

Best in industry benefits: HMO, remote work allowance, PTO, paid coworking, learning & development budget, 1x event experience per year.

A builder culture: space to experiment, propose ideas and shape how we make commercial decisions.

Exposure to multiple markets (UK, Europe, Asia, America) and categories (music, sports, premium events).

title: Content Creator – Scale a Global SaaS Company

 This is not a “post a few times per week” role. This is a growth-critical position, where your content will directly impact:

- Newsletters

- Landing pages (WordPress + Elementor)

 Everything is connected.

 

 One idea ? multiple formats ? multiple channels

- Write high-quality SEO blog posts

- Create LinkedIn posts that build authority

- Build and publish landing pages

Weekly Output Expectations

 

- 3–5 LinkedIn posts

- 1 newsletter

- Content repurposing across channels

 

What We’re Looking For

 

 You are:

Language & Market Approach

 

 

All content is created English-first

Then localized into Danish, Swedish, and Norwegian (as we scale)

Focus markets:

Denmark ? Sweden ? Norway ? Finland ? Netherlands ? Germany ? UK



Bonus:

 Important

 

We are not looking for someone who:

We are building fast.

 

- Execution speed matters.

 

 

What You Get

 

- Direct impact on a fast-growing SaaS company

- Work closely with founders

- Ownership of content & growth

- Freedom to execute ideas

- Opportunity to help build a global brand



Salary: Based on experience.

title: Full-Time Personal Assistant (Hybrid, Manila) — Founder Support Across Multiple Projects

I'm a founder running several active projects (mobile apps, web platforms, and personal ventures) and I need a sharp, reliable Personal Assistant to work alongside me day-to-day. This is a full-time, hybrid role based in Manila — you'll split time between working in person with me and handling tasks remotely. I don't need someone who just executes tasks — I need a thinking partner who can reason through problems, spot what I'm missing, and bring creative solutions to the table.



What you'll do



Manage my calendar, inbox, and daily schedule

Coordinate across my different projects — tracking deadlines, follow-ups, and deliverables

Handle personal errands and logistics (appointments, bookings, deliveries, light household coordination)

Draft
s, messages, and documents on my behalf

Research topics, vendors, tools, or people — and come back with a recommendation, not just raw info

Take notes in meetings and make sure nothing falls through the cracks

Use AI tools (especially Claude) as a core part of your workflow — drafting, summarizing, researching, brainstorming, automating repetitive work

Jump in on ad-hoc tasks — anything that frees up my time to focus on building

You're a great fit if you



Are based in Metro Manila and able to meet in person several days per week

Have 2+ years of experience as an EA, PA, or operations assistant

Are a strong critical thinker — you ask "why" before "how," and push back when something doesn't make sense

Are creative and resourceful — when you hit a wall, you find three ways around it

Actively use Claude (or ChatGPT, Gemini, etc.) in your daily work and can show me how

Write clear, professional English

Are extremely organized — Notion, Google Workspace, and task managers are second nature

Follow through without being reminded

Can keep information confidential and exercise good judgment

Are comfortable with technology and fast to pick up new tools

Nice to have



Experience supporting founders, executives, or creatives

Familiarity with project management tools (Linear, Asana, Trello, etc.)

Prompt engineering experience or a clear point of view on how to get the best out of AI

Background in admin, operations, or hospitality

Schedule



Full-time, Monday–Friday, with some flexibility

Hybrid: Some days in person in BGC, remainder remote

How to apply

Send me a short message with:



A 1-paragraph intro about yourself and why this role fits

A specific example of how you've used Claude or another AI tool to solve a real problem — what was the problem, how did you prompt it, what was the outcome?

A time you pushed back on a boss, client, or colleague and why you turned out to be right

The phrase "pancit canton" somewhere in your reply (so I know you read this)

title: Experienced Xero Accountant
description:
Stock2Cash is an Australian, tech-led Start-up in the E-commerce space. We are looking to hire to our core team an individual with a problem solver with a “Can do mentality to join as an account manager.



We are looking for an experienced and reliable accountant with strong Xero expertise to help manage and maintain our financial records as our business continues to grow.



This role requires someone who is comfortable working with high transaction volumes, reconciliations, and integrations, and who can keep our accounts accurate, organised, and up to date. You will work closely with the founder and operations team to ensure financial clarity and efficiency.



Key Responsibilities



-Manage and maintain company accounts in Xero

-Perform daily and monthly bank reconciliations

-Manage accounts payable and accounts receivable

-Review and maintain accurate financial records

-Prepare financial reports and summaries

-Assist with BAS / GST preparation and documentation (if required)

-Monitor and maintain correct categorisation of transactions

-Ensure all financial data is accurate and up to date

-Support integration of Xero with other platforms and payment systems



Requirements



-Proven experience using Xero in a professional accounting role

Strong understanding of reconciliation and financial controls

-Experience managing medium to high transaction volumes

-Excellent attention to detail and accuracy

-Ability to work independently and communicate clearly

-Reliable and organised with strong follow-through

-Highly Valued Experience

-Working with e-commerce or marketplace businesses

-Experience with payment gateways and platform integrations

-Experience preparing BAS and GST reporting in Australia



What We’re Looking For



We are looking for someone who goes beyond basic bookkeeping and can help keep our financial systems structured, transparent, and efficient as the business grows.



The successful candidate will have the unique opportunity to learn from and influence seasoned entrepreneurs who have built several successful companies. As you take on more responsibility, you will also have the chance to grow financially. Please add 'push boundaries' as your last word in your application or message to show your enthusiasm for this growth opportunity.




  is looking to build a fun, exceptional team, so you can expect to come in with a tonne of support and training. We’re fully set up to support our whole team working from home; we know the only thing scarier than starting a new job is starting a new job remotely, but we’ve got you ;)

As you take on more responsibility, you will also have the chance to grow financially. Please add 'push boundaries' as your last word in your application or message to show your enthusiasm for this growth opportunity.




 is looking to build a fun, exceptional team, so you can expect to come in with a ton of support and training. We’re fully set up to support our whole team working from home; we know the only thing scarier than starting a new job is starting a new job remotely, but we’ve got you ;)

title: DM Setter (Instagram) – Female Fat Loss Coaching Brand
description:
DM Setter (Instagram) – Female Fat Loss Coaching Brand



We’re hiring a serious, experienced DM Setter to convert new Instagram followers into booked calls for one of the world’s leading female fat loss coaching brands.



This is not an entry-level role. You’ll be expected to hit the ground running, manage conversations properly, and drive results daily.



The Role



Your job is simple:

Turn new followers - conversations - booked calls



You’ll be working primarily inside Go High Level, using:



Tagging systems

Follow-up pipelines

Daily messaging workflows



-Nothing slips through the cracks.

-Every lead is tracked.

-Every conversation is pushed forward.



-Hours & Pay

6 days per week

2pm – 10pm (UK time)

$5/hour



-Responsibilities

Respond to all new Instagram DMs quickly and effectively

Start conversations with new followers

Qualify leads properly (no time wasters booked)

Follow up relentlessly using GHL tagging system

Move conversations from cold ? warm ? booked call

Keep pipeline organised at all times



You MUST Have

Proven experience as an Instagram DM setter (fitness niche)

Strong understanding of sales psychology in DMs

Experience using Go High Level (GHL)

Confidence using tags, pipelines, and follow-up systems

Ability to handle high message volume daily

Fluent written English with a natural, conversational tone



Do NOT Apply If

You’ve never worked as an Instagram DM setter on a fitness page

You’ve never used Go High Level or don’t understand tagging systems

You need constant supervision or hand-holding

You’re slow to reply or inconsistent with work

You’re just “looking for experience”



What Makes This Different

High volume of warm inbound leads daily

Clear systems already in place

Opportunity to work inside a fast-growing, high-performing team

Direct impact on revenue (your work = booked calls)



How to Apply



Send:



Your experience as a DM setter (fitness niche only)

Screenshots or proof of results (booked calls, convos, etc.)

Your experience using Go High Level (be specific)

Why you’re a good fit for THIS role



If you’re good, you’ll make this role look easy.



If you’re not, it’ll expose you quickly.



Apply accordingly.

title: Client Success & Operations Manager
description:
We are an Australian business that helps organisations simplify the complexity of managing compliance and capability programs. We manage vendor relationships, team certifications, expiry dates, role changes, and program updates — all through one dashboard with a dedicated service support overlay. Our clients rely on us to make something genuinely complicated feel completely effortless.



We're a small, close-knit operation and we care deeply about the experience our clients have with us. Our best clients have been with us for years — and that's not by a
.





THE ROLE — Client Success & Operations Manager (Full-Time, Remote)



We're looking for someone who genuinely loves working with people and finds real satisfaction in building long-term relationships — and who is equally at home in the detail. You'll be the heart of our business — the person our clients hear from, trust, and rely on — while also being the engine behind the programs you manage.



This is not just a relationship role and it is not just an admin role. It is both — and the thread that connects them is data. You will develop a deep understanding of each client's program data, translate that data into clear summaries and actionable insights, and work directly with clients to action those insights together. You don't just report what the data says — you understand what it means, what needs to happen next, and you drive that forward in partnership with your clients.



You'll work closely with our Systems & Data Administrator, who ensures all data is clean, accurate, and ready to use. Together you'll analyse program data and build client action plans. You're the face of the team. They're the foundation you build on.





WHAT YOU'LL DO



Client relationship management

— Be the warm, professional, reliable voice of our business for our clients

— Build and nurture long-term client relationships through consistent, proactive communication

— Manage day-to-day client interactions via
 and calls

— Proactively flag upcoming renewals, changes, and action items before clients need to ask

— Handle contract renewals with confidence and a genuine interest in client success

— Provide a responsive, warm, and highly professional service experience at all times



Program administration and reporting

— Update and maintain client compliance and capability programs in our client-facing dashboard

— Process adds, moves, and changes across vendor lists, tea
mbers, certifications, and roles

— Monitor and track certification expiry dates and proactively manage renewals

— Run regular reports for clients and internal use

— Manage program changes as directed by clients and ensure the dashboard reflects real-time accuracy at all times

— Maintain meticulous records across all client accounts — nothing is ever missed



Data analysis, insights and client action planning

— Develop a deep understanding of each client's program data — not just what it shows, but what it means

— Work closely with our Systems & Data Administrator to review, interrogate, and analyse program data regularly

— Identify trends, gaps, upcoming expirations, risks, and opportunities across client programs

— Prepare clear, concise data summaries for each client that cut through complexity and highlight what matters

— Translate data insights into specific, actionable recommendations — never just observations

— Present insights to clients in a confident, clear, and compelling way

— Work in consultation and conjunction with clients to action those recommendations together — you don't hand over a report and walk away, you see it through

— Track the outcomes of actions taken and feed learnings back into future planning

— Communicate client priorities and feedback back to the systems team to ensure programs stay current and relevant





WHO YOU ARE



— You love people and find genuine joy in building lasting professional relationships

— You are warm, empathetic, and clients naturally feel at ease with you

— You are highly organised and thrive in a role that requires both people skills and precision

— You have a genuine affinity for data — you enjoy digging into it, understanding it deeply, and turning it into insights that mean something

— You are not satisfied with surface-level reporting — you want to know what the data is really saying and what should happen as a result

— You are proactive — you anticipate what clients need before they ask

— You communicate beautifully in English, both written and spoken — including the ability to present complex data simply and compellingly

— You notice the small things and follow through without being reminded

— You are calm, reliable, and thrive when managing multiple clients and moving parts simultaneously

— You are a natural collaborator who understands that great client outcomes are built on great teamwork

— You have experience in client services, account management, operations, or a similar role combining people, data, and administration

— You are confident using dashboards, reporting tools, and spreadsheets — and you pick up new systems quickly

— Experience in compliance, certifications, or vendor management is a strong advantage — but we will absolutely train the right person





WHAT WE OFFER



— Full-time remote role — 40 hours per week, Monday to Friday

— Salary: $1,500 USD per month

— 13th month bonus paid in December

— A stable, long-term position — we value loyalty and reward it

— A close-knit team environment where collaboration is genuine and your contribution is valued

— A genuinely supportive working environment where your contribution is seen and appreciated

— Day to day autonomy — we expect you to take the lead and have a genuine sense of ownership and responsibility for your role

— Direct access to the business owner — no layers of bureaucracy

— The opportunity to truly own this role, grow within it, and make it yours

— The opportunity for career growth





HOW TO APPLY



Please send your CV along with a short paragraph of 5–8 sentences telling us specifically why this role speaks to you and why you'd be wonderful at it. We are not looking for a generic cover letter — we want to hear your voice and understand who you are and why and how you'd make this role a success.



Applications without this paragraph will not be considered.



We genuinely look forward to hearing from you.

title: Full time Reels/Shorts Editor
description:
We are looking for a high-level short-form video editor to work directly with my personal brand and my education company, HighTicketElites.



This role is not about random viral edits or flashy effects.



Your job is to turn raw footage into clear, authoritative, scroll-stopping short-form content that builds trust, positions expertise, and converts attention into action.



You will be editing content used for:

 • Personal brand growth

 • Educational authority content

 • Paid ads (Meta, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts)

 • Lifestyle and cinematic b-roll reels

 • High-ticket offer promotion



This is a long-term position for someone who wants stability, growth, and to be part of a serious brand.



?



Responsibilities



Core Editing Responsibilities

 • Edit Instagram Reels, TikToks, and YouTube Shorts daily

 • Turn raw talking-head, voiceover, and b-roll footage into clean, high-retention short-form videos

 • Add on-screen captions optimized for mobile viewing and fast comprehension

 • Structure edits for:

 • Strong hooks in the first 1–3 seconds

 • Clear pacing and flow

 • Retention and watch time

 • Authority and clarity, not gimmicks

 • Deliver edits that work for both organic content and paid ads



Creative & Brand Alignment

 • Follow specific content frameworks and creative direction

 • Maintain a clean, premium, masculine, authority-driven style

 • Understand how to edit content differently for:

 • Educational value

 • Lifestyle branding

 • Conversion-focused ads

 • Match pacing and tone to the message (calm, confident, disciplined)



Workflow & Communication

 • Edit content in batches and hit daily/weekly deadlines

 • Accept feedback quickly and implement revisions without friction

 • Organize files and exports cleanly

 • Communicate clearly and professionally



?



Required Skills & Experience



Must-Have

 • Proven experience editing short-form vertical content

 • Strong understanding of hook structure, pacing, and retention

 • Clean caption styling (readable, well-timed, no clutter)

 • Ability to edit both:

 • Talking-head videos

 • Cinematic b-roll / lifestyle footage

 • Fluent in English

 • Comfortable working with feedback and creative direction

 • Reliable internet and consistent availability



Tools

 • Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or CapCut Pro (any is fine if results are strong)

 • Familiarity with:

 • Instagram Reels

 • TikTok

 • YouTube Shorts formatting



?



Style We Are NOT Looking For



This role is not for editors who rely on:

 • Overused 
 edits

 • Excessive emojis

 • Loud sound effects

 • Cheap “viral” gimmicks

 • Random transitions with no purpose



We prioritize:

 • Clarity

 • Authority

 • Simplicity

 • Psychological flow

 • Premium feel



?



Output Expectations

 • 3–6 short-form videos per day (depending on length and complexity)

 • Consistent quality across all edits

 • Fast turnaround without sacrificing quality



?



Compensation

 • Competitive full-time monthly salary (based on experience)

 • Long-term opportunity with stable workload

 • Performance-based raises and bonuses for strong contributors



?



Growth Opportunity



This is a long-term role.

As the brand scales, top editors can grow into:

 • Lead Editor

 • Creative Director support

 • Ads-focused editing specialist



?



How to Apply



When applying, please include:

 1. A short introduction about yourself

 2. Your short-form editing experience

 3. Links to reels, shorts, or TikTok edits you’ve done

 4. The word “DISCIPLINE” at the top of your application so we know you read this fully



Applications without relevant short-form examples will not be considered.

title: AI Enabled Software Developer / Automation Systems Developer
description:
I’m hiring a full time AI Enabled Software Developer / Automation Systems Developer to help me improve and expand my internal software systems.

title: Project Coordinator
description:
I’m looking for a dependable and organized Virtual Project Coordinator to help me manage our overnight installation projects.



This role is all about communication and coordination. You won’t be making big decisions — your main responsibility is to keep projects moving smoothly, support our field installers through group chats, and escalate issues when needed.



If you’re someone who’s calm under pressure, detail-oriented, and good at managing conversations, this might be a great fit for you.



What You’ll Be Doing:



Managing overnight projects via group chats with installers

Guiding the team using provided layouts, documents, and videos

Monitoring progress and flagging any issues right away

Escalating concerns to me or the client for approval

Providing updates when clients are included in chats

During slower times, posting previous project content on our social media

Helping set up ads for future posts (a plus if you have design/marketing experience)



What I’m Looking For:



Experience as a Project Coordinator, VA, or Operations Support

Strong written English communication

Organized and detail-focused

Comfortable juggling multiple conversations

Reliable and responsive

Willing to work US night hours (Day shift in PH) long-term



Schedule:



Full-time

US Night Shift (this is permanent)

First 1–3 months will follow US time strictly for training



I’m looking for someone who wants stability and long-term work. If that’s you, please send your resume and a short intro about your experience.



 me your updated resume at 
 with your intro voice recording via vocaroo use subject line: "POUR"



Excited to meet the right person!

title: 24 HR Review | VA Remote Virtual Receptionist
description:
Important: Our application process moves quickly. If you are selected to proceed to the next interview stages, you must be able to respond promptly. Candidates who do not respond in a timely manner may be passed over.



We are currently seeking a Remote Spa Receptionist to manage inbound calls and book spa appointments. This is a long-term, performance-based position. Calls are recorded and reviewed regularly. Continued placement in this role depends on accuracy, professionalism, booking success, reliable attendance, no missed calls during scheduled shifts, quick response times, and the ability to professionally manage complaints and de-escalate dissatisfied clients.



To apply: Please copy the questions below, paste them into your reply, and answer each question thoroughly.



(1) Location & Utilities: Where in the Philippines are you currently located? How often have you experienced power or internet outages in the past year? Do you have backup solutions (e.g., generator, mobile hotspot) to ensure your work is not affected?



(2) Home Setup: Will you be working from home? Is your workspace quiet and suitable for handling calls? Do you have your own computer or laptop? Please attach a screenshot of your current internet speed.



(3) Availability & Speed: Can you consistently work seven days a week, six hours per day, and remain available for the full shift without missed calls? How quickly do you typically answer inbound calls? What is your current typing speed in words per minute?



(4) Call & Complaint Experience: Describe your experience with answering inbound calls, booking or scheduling appointments, customer service, following scripts, and handling complaints. Provide one real example of how you de-escalated an upset client and the outcome.



(5) Tools & Systems: What programs, tools, phone systems, or VoIP platforms have you used for handling calls? Do you have any issues with background noise, power outages, or internet interruptions? How do you take notes and log client interactions during calls?



(6) Voice Recordings: Please submit two short voice recordings. Recording one should demonstrate booking an appointment from 7:00 PM to 8:00 PM with a total amount of $60. Recording two should demonstrate handling a double charge for a 90-minute massage where the client sees two $90 charges and is requesting a refund. Your tone should be calm, polite, and professional.



(7) Current Job: Do you currently have another job? If yes, where, how many hours per week, and what are your roles and tasks in that job?



(8) Schedule, Pay & Start Date: Are you able to work seven days a week, six hours per day, during Central Standard Time (CST) hours without conflicts? What is your expected hourly or monthly rate? What is your earliest physical start date if hired?



(9) Marketing, Design & Video: Do you have experience with online marketing, creating content using Canva, or producing short video clips such as
 Reels or similar platforms? Please describe your skills and provide samples of your previous work if available.



(10) Fit, Skills & Experience: Briefly introduce yourself and explain why you would be a good fit for this role. Include what you believe is most important when handling spa clients over the phone, especially during complaints, and what other skills or areas you have a lot of experience in.

title: Experienced Sales Funnel Builder (Funnelish)
description:
Hours: Full-time (8 hours/day, Monday–Friday) Salary: 60,000 PHP/month + 10% profit share on successful funnels (uncapped earning potential)



About the Role

We are a growing e-commerce company running direct-response funnels across multiple European markets. We are looking for a skilled, reliable, and proactive Virtual Assistant to join our team long-term and take ownership of building and localizing our sales funnels.

If you are a fast, detail-oriented funnel builder who loves good copy, clean design, and getting things done without being micromanaged — please keep reading.



What You Will Be Doing

Building complete sales funnels in Funnelish, including advertorials, sales pages, checkout pages, and upsells — based on existing funnels we provide as templates.

Translating and adapting our funnels into new markets using DeepL and Claude (we will teach you our process).

Adapting competitor funnel images and creating new product visuals using ChatGPT image generation and Artlist (Nano Banana Pro) so they match our product and branding.

Communicating daily in English with your manager (me) — we work closely together, so responsiveness and a proactive attitude are essential. When changes are needed, I expect them to be made quickly and correctly.



Minimum Requirements (please do not apply if you do not meet these)

Perfect English, both written and spoken. Our entire workflow and copywriting depends on strong English skills.

Proven experience with AI tools like Claude and ChatGPT — you use them daily and know how to prompt well.

You have personally built at least 3 full sales funnels in the past (not just edited pages — complete funnels from advertorial to upsell).

Strong eye for design and copy details. You notice when something is off.

Reliable internet connection and the ability to work full-time (8 hrs/day, 5 days/week). If you are someone who does not show up during working hours, this is not the right job for you.



How To Apply

To be considered, your application must include the following:

A PDF motivation letter explaining why you are the perfect fit for this role. Tell me about yourself, your experience, and why this job excites you.

Links to or PDF example of at least 3 sales funnels you have personally built. I want to see your actual work — advertorials, sales pages, checkouts, upsells. Please specify what exactly you built on each funnel.

Applications that do not include both a PDF motivation letter and 3 funnel examples will be ignored.



What's In It For You

A stable, long-term full-time job at 60,000 PHP/month.

10% profit-sharing bonus on every successful funnel you build. The better your work, the more you earn — salary is uncapped which means that it is possible for you to earn twice, or even triple the base salary.

Work directly with the owner, learn our proven direct-response system, and grow with the company.

Clear expectations, fast feedback, and real ownership of your work.



I look forward to reading your application. Serious, experienced applicants only, please.

title: E-commerce Content Strategist
description:
Job Title: Content Specialist / Senior Copywriter | Remote



Location: Asia or similar time zones (UTC +8)

Salary: $2,000-$2,500/month gross

Schedule: Full-time, 40 hours/week



Only English CVs will be accepted.



Retalent Agency is a global remote recruitment agency that connects talented professionals like you with top companies in e-commerce, marketing, and tech. Since 2019, we’ve helped hundreds of candidates from Latin America, Eastern Europe, Asia, and South Africa land full-time remote roles with international teams.



When you apply through Retalent, you’re not just applying for a job; you’re stepping into a community of growing businesses and ambitious professionals.



Let us help you find the right opportunity to grow your career from anywhere in the world.



Our Client:



Founded by two sisters who grew up navigating eczema and fungal skin infections within their own family, this brand was built on a deeply personal mission. Drawing from heritage botanical wisdom and combining it with research-backed science, they create potent, plant-powered skin remedies that genuinely make a difference.



With over 1.5 million products delivered, 25,000+ five-star reviews, and a decade of customer trust, they have established themselves as a beloved name in natural skincare. Now, they are expanding into new channels and audiences to reach even more families who need their products.



They are a small, US-based, globally distributed team that believes great content is how great products find the people who need them. Everything they create lives by one standard: Ingredients Matter: Pure, Potent, Proven.



In This Role, You’ll:



Act as a sharp, detail-oriented content writer with a strong sense of brand voice and consumer appeal. This is a content-focused role built around your ability to research deeply, write compelling copy across multiple formats, and serve as the quality gate for all customer-facing content.



You'll work closely with our co-founders and Assistant Marketing Manager as part of a globally distributed team. AI tools are central to how we work, you'll use them to accelerate research, generate drafts, and iterate quickly. Your judgment, brand instinct, and editorial eye are what make the output great.



This is a full-time, remote position with working hours that substantially overlap UTC+8. How you communicate and collaborate matters as much as your talent and qualifications.



What You'll Be Doing



-Content Creation: Write scripts for TikTok branded videos, Amazon video ads, and future ad platforms. Create
 copy, website content, educational articles for AEO/ GEO visibility, and long-form content.

-Creative Testing & Messaging: Develop systematic messaging variations for messaging and creative testing, breaking down product angles, benefits, and hooks to identify what resonates through data.

-Content Review & Refinement: Serve as the brand voice quality gate. Review and refine output from AI tools and third-party platforms (such as Amazon listing optimization tools) for accuracy, consumer appeal, and brand alignment.

-Research & Consumer Insights: Conduct deep product, market, and competitive research. Mine customer reviews, forums, and social conversations to understand real customer language, pain points, and motivations. Use these insights to shape stronger messaging.

-Content Repurposing & Social Media: Adapt existing content (video scripts,
 copy, product messaging, articles) into social media posts across Instagram,
 
, and other platforms.

-Creative Briefing: Write clear, actionable briefs for graphic designers and TikTok content creators to keep visual and verbal messaging aligned and on-brand.

-Additional Marketing Support: Contribute to other marketing initiatives as needed, including Amazon customer Q&A, review engagement, and emerging projects that support the company’s growth objectives.



What You’ll Bring:



-2 to 4 years of experience in content writing, copywriting, or brand communications, ideally for U.S.-facing consumer brands.

-Strong written English with the ability to produce polished, consumer-facing copy that sounds natural to a U.S. audience. Fluent in spoken English.

-Demonstrated ability to write across formats: video scripts, product copy,
, social media, website, and long-form articles.

-Skilled at prompting AI writing and research tools (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT, or similar) to produce high-quality output. AI is a core part of our content workflow, not an occasional aid.

-Strong research skills and instinct. You dig into products, competitors, reviews, social signals, and customer conversations before you start writing.

-A portfolio or writing samples that demonstrate brand voice adaptability and consumer-oriented messaging.

-Familiarity with Amazon e-commerce, health and wellness, or OTC skin products is a plus but not required.



Skill Sets



-Excellent writing and editing skills with a sharp editorial eye for both accuracy and consumer appeal.

-Research-driven mindset. You investigate before you write.

-Structured creative thinking. Able to break a single product into multiple testable angles, benefits, and hooks.

-You can internalize a brand's tone and execute it consistently across formats.

-Collaborative, low-ego, and self-directed. You take high-level direction and run with it.

-Intellectually honest with data. You let performance results guide the next iteration.

-Welcomes direct feedback and iterates quickly.

-Fast learner motivated to grow into new areas of marketing beyond content.



What’s In It for You:



-Competitive salary: $2,000 to $2,500 USD per month.

-Competitive raise based on performance.

-Paid vacation.

-Professional development opportunities.

-Opportunity to grow into a senior marketing role as the team and brand expand.

-Be part of a close-knit and supportive team that values your input and relies on your expertise.

-Collaboration without micromanagement.

-Work-life balance.
