title: Executive Assistant — LinkedIn Growth, Prospecting & Operations (US Financial Services)
description:
Please read the full post before applying. Generic applications will be rejected.



ABOUT US



Equity Check is a capital aggregation platform that helps US wealth managers and financial advisors access institutional-grade private real estate. Our founder has raised over $550M from 2,000+ investors in 15 years. We work with proven operators like Venterra (18.5% avg IRR, zero investor capital lost across 83 completed deals).



We are hiring an Executive Assistant to own two areas of our business:



1. LINKEDIN GROWTH ENGINE

Build a LinkedIn-driven pipeline targeting 200 US wealth managers who each allocate $5M+ per year to private investments. This is an AI-leveraged business development role, not a social media posting role. You will work alongside the founder, not for him.



2. EXECUTIVE OPERATIONS

Inbox triage, meeting prep, Notion project management, scheduling, and general right-hand support across all Equity Check initiatives.



WHAT YOU WILL DO



- Content production: 5 LinkedIn posts per week, biweekly newsletter, weekly Canva carousel (drafts go to founder for approval)

- Engagement: 75-100 substantive comments per week on prospect posts

- Prospecting: 50-60 personalized LinkedIn connection requests per week using a 4-step warm-up sequence

- CRM: Log every prospect interaction in Attio CRM. Pipeline discipline is essential.

- AI workflows: Build and refine AI workflows using Claude and ChatGPT for content drafting, prospect research, comment drafting, and meeting prep. Prompt engineering is a core skill for this role.

- Executive support: Draft
 replies, prepare meeting briefs, manage calendar, run Notion project tracking, follow through on anything that needs an owner.



THE ONLY METRIC THAT MATTERS



2-3 qualified wealth manager meetings booked on the founder's calendar per week. Everything else supports that number.



COMPENSATION



- Base: $800-1,600/month part-time, scaling to $1,600-2,800/month full-time based on performance (typical 3-6 month progression)

- Performance bonus: $500-1,500 USD for each wealth manager you source who becomes an active investor with us. Realistic Year 1 bonus upside: $5,000-15,000+ on top of base.

- Long-term upside: Uncapped share of backend revenue on allocations you sourced, paid over 3-5 years.



MUST HAVE



- Excellent written English (native-level, not translated)

- Proven LinkedIn management experience for a founder, executive, or business (must share specific metrics and examples)

- Experience as a right-hand EA or operations support for an executive (calendar, inbox, project management), not just marketing

- Strong fluency with Claude AI and/or ChatGPT for daily work, with a real point of view on where AI helps vs. where it damages authenticity

- CRM experience (Attio, HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar) with strong pipeline discipline

- Comfortable with direct, unvarnished feedback and performance-based compensation



NICE TO HAVE



- Experience with US financial services, real estate, or private investment clients

- Notion, Canva, Descript, HeyReach, AuthoredUp, Sales Navigator, or Clay experience

- Prior work with a solo founder or small team (not big corporate EA)



HOW TO APPLY



In your application, send the following. Applications without these three items will be rejected:



1. Link to a LinkedIn account you have managed (yours or a client's) with specific metrics you drove (follower growth, engagement rate, leads, etc.)

2. One specific change you would recommend for the founder's LinkedIn profile (
/in/andrew-james-davis-cre/) in one sentence. Be specific, not generic.

3. Describe one AI workflow (Claude or ChatGPT) you have built and used repeatedly. Share the prompt structure, the output, and what you edit every time before using it.



I take hiring seriously. You should too.

title: Marketing Associate - Twilio, A2P, and SMS compliance
description:
!!! PLEASE READ THE ENTIRE JOB POST BEFORE APPLYING !!!



Marketing Associate for SMS Compliance (PH)



We’re looking for an A2P &
 Deliverability Operations Specialist to help build and scale the core communication infrastructure that powers Moxie’s provider network.



This role combines strong operational discipline with a technical mindset. You’ll own the systems that ensure providers can reliably reach their patients—from A2P SMS registration and Twilio verification to Mailgun setup and
 deliverability.



You won’t just follow a process—you'll help build and improve it. From fixing DNS issues to creating trackers and exploring automation, your work will directly impact how quickly providers launch and how effectively they communicate.



This is a great role for someone who loves structured systems, catching details others miss, and making workflows run smoothly at scale.



What you’ll do:



- Own A2P registration end-to-end: Manage the full 10DLC/A2P process for all providers — from submission to approval — ensuring fast turnaround times and high-quality applications.

- Manage CNAM and SHAKEN/STIR registrations: Ensure accurate caller ID display and improved call deliverability by managing CNAM and SHAKEN/STIR setup within Twilio for all client accounts.

- Manage
 setup and deliverability: Set up and maintain Mailgun accounts, configure DNS (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), and ensure domains are properly authenticated and ready to send.

- Drive domain warming processes: Manage
 warming (e.g., via Warmy) so providers launch with strong sender reputations and avoid deliverability issues.

- Reduce provider launch friction: Identify and fix bottlenecks that delay SMS or
 readiness, helping providers go live faster.

- Build visibility into systems: Create and maintain trackers or dashboards that show real-time status of A2P registrations,
 setup, and domain health.

- Systematize and improve workflows: Turn repeatable processes into clear SOPs and continuously improve them—including exploring automation and AI tools.

- Collaborate with internal teams: Work closely with Provider Success and Marketing to ensure communication setup is fully integrated into onboarding and campaign launches.

- Become the go-to expert: Act as the internal resource for A2P and
 deliverability — troubleshooting issues, spotting patterns, and improving outcomes over time.



What we’re looking for:



- Strong attention to detail: experience working with systems where accuracy is important, like DNS, configurations, or technical setups.

- Hands-on experience with tools like Twilio, Mailgun, or similar SMS/email platforms.

- Experience or strong interest in marketing operations,
 systems, or technical workflows.

- Bonus if you’ve worked with DNS configuration (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) or domain/email deliverability.

- Bonus if you’ve built workflows using tools like Zapier, Make, n8n, or similar.

- Problem-solving mindset — you can troubleshoot issues and figure things out independently.

- High ownership — you proactively identify gaps and follow through without being told.

- Clear communicator, especially in async/remote environments.



Why join us?



At Moxie, communication infrastructure isn’t just backend work — it’s a critical part of how our providers grow.



If you’re someone who enjoys making systems work, solving technical problems, and building processes that actually scale, this role was designed for you.



To Apply, please use this link:

title: Creative Strategist – High-Performance Meta Ads (DTC / Dropshipping)
description:
WHO WE ARE



We run a seven-figure direct-response e-commerce business selling branded products across Europe and the UK. Our entire growth engine runs on paid ads — specifically Meta. We don't make pretty content. We make ads that convert, scale, and drive revenue.



We are hiring a second Creative Strategist to expand the creative team and hold the standard of output as we scale.





THE ROLE



This is 90% creative strategy and team management, 10?sic Meta media buying. You will own the creative pipeline end to end — research, concepts, briefs, feedback, iteration, and performance review. You will also be responsible for finding and managing video editing talent as the team grows.





WHAT YOU WILL DO



Develop concepts, angles, and hooks that drive profitable customer acquisition.



Write ad scripts and copy that convert 



Create detailed briefs for designers and video editors, review output, and approve final ads.



Manage the creative testing pipeline — always have the next batch ready.



Analyze ad performance weekly: what is winning, what is dying, what to test next.



Mine competitor ad libraries, Reddit, reviews, and comments for real customer language and hooks.



Build and maintain a creative knowledge base — document what works and feed it into the next round of briefs.



Use Claude and AI tools as a core part of your workflow, not a shortcut.



Help identify and onboard new video editing talent when needed.



Handle basic Meta campaign management — enough to read the data and understand what is scaling.





OUR VALUES



Geniuses and Owners. We work with builders who see a problem and fix it. Not people waiting to be told what to do.



Iterations over Hours. We do not measure success by desk time. We measure it by how fast you test, learn, and improve. Ship it. Learn. Repeat.



Truth over Ego. No politics. No BS. Honesty and clarity, even when it is uncomfortable. Just data and results.





WHO YOU ARE



You have real experience running or analyzing Meta and
 ads. This is non-negotiable.



You understand direct-response marketing deeply, not just surface-level ad trends.



You know how to write copy that converts, not just sounds good.



You are fluent with Claude and AI tools — not "I have tried ChatGPT," but actual workflow integration.



You understand customer psychology, awareness levels, hooks, and angles.



You are familiar with key metrics: CTR, CPA, CVR, ROAS, hook rate, hold rate.



You are organized and proactive. You close loops without being chased.



You are comfortable advertising men sex toys - in a compliant way with a content you can actually see on
.



Bonus: knowledge of Cialdini, Eugene Schwartz, or Gary Bencivenga.





THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR YOU IF



You want a safe monthly salary with no accountability to results.



You rely entirely on AI to do the thinking for you.



You care more about visuals than messaging.



You have no experience with real ad spend.



You are not willing to go deep into an ad account and handle basic media buying tasks.



You are not hungry to learn fast and improve continuously.



COMPENSATION

 

Base salary

 

On top of that, you earn a performance bonus directly tied to the ads you create. For every creative that hits $3,000 in ad spend at a strong ROAS 2.0, you earn $100. There is no cap.

 

To put it in real numbers: if you produce 10 winning creatives in a month, that is an extra $1,000 on top of your base. And that is a conservative month. Strong performers will earn significantly more.

 

For those who perform consistently, we move to an even better structure — a direct percentage from profit on the ads you create. The upside grows with you.

 

HOW TO APPLY



Show us proof, not a portfolio.



We receive a lot of applications. The only ones we read carefully are those that show real work with real results. Here is exactly what we need:

SHARE A LOOM VIDEO WITH:

1. Your Best created add- We need the actual creative, not a description of it.



2. Your brief or script of add. This is how we know you made it, not just reviewed it. No brief means your application will not be considered.



3. A performance screenshot for add. Spend, ROAS, CTR — whatever you have. It does not need to be perfect numbers, just proof it ran.  



4. 2 to 3 sentences per ad explaining the angle and why it worked



5. What do you like about creative strategy and what you are currently looking for?



6. Attention check: add the words "War Strawberry" at the top of your application. Applications without it will not be reviewed.



P.S. We are looking for real, thoughtful applications — not copy-paste responses. If your application was clearly written by AI, it will not be considered. If your English is not perfect but your work is genuine, that is completely fine. If you read this far you are ahead of 90% applicants so apply for the role.

title: Claude AI Specialist
description:
Kimbrough Legal, PLLC



About the Role



We are a family law firm in the US looking for a skilled Claude AI Specialist to help us set up, integrate, and optimize Claude across our daily operations. You will be our go-to person for everything Claude-related, from building custom workflows and prompts to training our team on how to use the tool effectively.

Our firm runs on the Microsoft ecosystem (Microsoft 365, Outlook, Teams, Dropbox, Monday, and MyCase).



This is a hands-on technical role. You will be the backbone of our AI adoption, so we are looking for someone who genuinely knows Claude inside and out.



What You’ll Do

• Set up and configure Claude (Pro/Team/Enterprise) for our firm

• Design and build custom Projects, Styles, and Skills tailored to legal workflows

• Create and refine prompt templates for document review, drafting, research, and client communication

• Integrate Claude with our current tech stack using API

• Build internal automations and agents using Claude Code, the Claude API, or Power Automate where useful

• Train our team

• Document everything so our team can self-serve

• Keep us updated on new Claude features and recommend improvements



Must-Have Skills

• Strong hands-on experience with Claude (Projects, Artifacts, MCP connectors, Claude Code, API)

• Solid understanding of prompt engineering and context management

• Workflow automation experience (Power Automate, Zapier, Make, n8n, or custom scripts)

• Comfortable working with APIs and basic scripting (Python or JavaScript preferred)

• Excellent written English

• Able to explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical users



Nice to Have

• Experience working with law firms or other professional services

• Familiarity with legal tech tools that integrate with Microsoft (MyCase, Monday, and etc.)

• Background in document management or knowledge management systems

• Experience with Microsoft Copilot for comparison/integration purposes

• Experience with other AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini)



What We Offer

• $8-$10 USD/hour, paid in US dollars

• Full-time hours (40/week)

• Long-term, stable role with a growing firm

• Opportunity to shape how AI is used across the entire company



How to Apply

Please send:

1. A short intro about yourself and your Claude experience

2. Links or screenshots of Claude projects, automations, or prompts you’ve built.

3. Your earliest start date



Screening question (please answer in your application): Describe one workflow you built using Claude that saved meaningful time for a team or client. What made it work?



Applications without an answer to the screening question will not be considered.

title: Jr Sales Associate
description:
Who We Are

Charlotte Party Rentals is a family-owned tent solutions company founded in 2006 in Charlotte NC. We have completed 5,000+ structured event setups for corporate clients, construction companies, festivals, weddings, and community organizations across the Carolinas. We hold a 4.9-star Google rating across 174 reviews.



We are an AI-first company. We use Claude AI, GoHighLevel CRM, and digital tools across every function of the business. We are growing our sales team and looking for someone who is early in their sales career and wants to build real skills in a fast-moving, systems-driven environment.



The Role

This is an entry-level sales support role on our inbound sales team. You will work alongside our Senior Closer to move warm leads through the pipeline — qualifying, quoting, following up, and learning how to close. This role is designed to develop you into a full Closer within 6–12 months based on performance.



You are not expected to walk in as a polished closer. You are expected to show up with urgency, coachability, and a genuine drive to learn the business. CPR will train you on our products, quoting system, and sales process. You bring the discipline and the desire to grow.



Speed, follow-through, and pipeline discipline are the foundation of this role. You will be responsible for keeping the CRM clean, responding to leads fast, and executing the follow-up cadence on every open quote.



What You Will Do Every Day

• Speed to lead — Respond to all inbound leads within 4 hours of arrival — ask qualifying questions: event type, guest count, date, site location, and budget range

• Quote support — Gather the event details needed for the Senior Closer or yourself to build an accurate quote using our Event Setup Proposal format

• Follow-up cadence — Execute the follow-up cadence on every open quote: day 1, day 3, and day 7

• Closing development — As you develop, take full ownership of qualifying and quoting smaller deals independently

• Pipeline hygiene — Keep the CRM updated daily — every deal has a status, next action, and close date

• Lost deal tracking — Log every lost deal with a reason before marking it lost

• Reporting — Send a Friday summary of your pipeline: open quotes, follow-ups completed, and any deals moved forward or lost

• AI-first workflow — Use AI tools including Claude to draft follow-up messages, research clients, and improve communication speed and quality



What This Role Is Not

• This is not a lead generation or cold outreach role — leads come to you

• This is not a passive order-taking role — you are expected to learn and earn responsibility

• This is not a part-time role — 40 hours per week, full commitment

• This is not a role for someone who waits to be told what to do next

• This is not a stepping stone to something unrelated — we are investing in you as a future Closer



Compensation



Base salary $500 – $700 USD/month (Based on experience — increases after 90-day review)

Commission To be discussed

Schedule Charlotte NC business hours (Eastern Time overlap required)

Start date Immediately (Right candidate starts as soon as possible)





What We Are Looking For

Required

• Strong written and spoken English — you communicate with US clients daily via
, SMS, and phone

• Basic CRM familiarity — any platform. You do not need to be an expert; you need to be disciplined about using it

• Some sales or client-facing experience — retail, hospitality, customer service, or any role where you had to persuade or guide someone

• Tech fluency — comfortable learning new software quickly and using digital tools day to day

• Availability during Charlotte NC business hours — Eastern Time overlap is required

• Coachability — you take direction, apply feedback, and improve week over week



Preferred (Not Required)

• Exposure to event, hospitality, or rental industry environments

• Familiarity with GoHighLevel or similar CRM platforms

• Experience using AI tools like Claude or ChatGPT in a work setting

• Experience supporting senior sales staff or working in a team sales environment



Who Will Thrive in This Role

You are the right person for this role if:



• You check your pipeline first thing every morning and do not need to be reminded

• You treat a warm lead that goes cold as a personal challenge

• You log every interaction in the CRM automatically — not when asked

• You want to grow into a full Closer and you are willing to earn that position

• You take feedback without getting defensive and apply it the same day

• You see AI tools as a competitive advantage, not a shortcut

• You want to be paid based on what you produce as you develop



Who Will Not Thrive in This Role

Do not apply if:



• You need daily reminders to follow up on open deals

• You treat CRM updates as optional

• You are looking for a purely passive support role with no accountability for outcomes

• You are not available during US Eastern Time business hours

• You are not open to coaching and direct feedback on your performance



Growth Path

The Jr Sales Associate role is a defined on-ramp to the full Remote Sales Closer position. Promotion is based on performance, not time. Here is what that path looks like:



• Days 1–30: Onboarding, CRM training, shadowing Senior Closer, handling lead intake and initial qualifying

• Days 31–90: Taking ownership of full quote-to-follow-up cycle on qualifying deals under supervision

• Day 91 review: Performance evaluation — base increase available, commission tier review, eligibility for Closer promotion discussed

• Promotion to Remote Sales Closer: Full pipeline ownership, higher commission tiers, and uncapped earning potential



How to Apply

We do not want a cover letter. We want to understand who you are and what you have done.



Send the following to 
 with the subject line: Jr Sales Associate Application — [Your Name]



• Your sales or client-facing experience — what role, what you were responsible for, and what you learned

• One example of a time you had to follow up persistently to get a result

• What CRM or tools you have used, even casually

• What AI tools you have used in any context — work or personal

• Your availability — what hours can you cover Eastern Time?

• Your CV or resume — a simple one is fine, we want to see your work history at a glance

• A 60–90 second video introduction — record on your phone, no production required. Tell us who you are, why this role, and what makes you someone who follows through. Send a link (Google Drive, Loom, YouTube unlisted, or similar)



Applications without a real answer to these questions will not be reviewed. We are not looking for perfection — we are looking for someone who is honest about where they are and hungry to grow.



Final screening includes one scenario question: A client calls and says they need a tent for a 75-person birthday party. What are the first three questions you ask them and why? We are looking for curiosity and client focus — not product knowledge.



About the Team

Charlotte Party Rentals is a family-owned business led by Chris Epps. Our operations team — including our Social Media and GHL Manager and our Client Relations Manager — is based in the Philippines. This is a proven structure that works for us. We value ownership, urgency, and people who treat the business like it is their own. We are building something and we want the right people building it with us.

title: Inventory & Operations Specialist
description:
IMPORTANT NOTE

Distilled Strategy is a 10099-based / contractor workforce. As a contractor, you are solely responsible for managing and filing your own taxes in accordance with your local jurisdiction's regulations. You will be required to work US hours (EST/PST) regardless of your location.



About Distilled Strategy

Distilled Strategy is a high-velocity design and strategy partner for the world’s most complex industries: Fintech, HealthTech, and B2B SaaS. We help startups and enterprises move from archaic and clunky to intuitive and fundable. Our work has helped clients raise over $170M+, and we pair strategic design with emerging tools—AI included—to modernize how businesses operate and grow.



Who We Are

We’re a gregarious crew of designers, strategists, and developers. We believe you produce your best work when given the freedom to work with a team you’d actually love to hang out with "off the clock". We take our work seriously, but not ourselves. 



Role Overview

The core mission of the Inventory & Operations Strategist is to serve as the "accountant" for physical goods, providing the operational backbone for supply chain and cash allocation. You are the single point of accountability for maintaining a rolling 90-day inventory churn, ensuring the company never goes out of stock while preventing excess capital from being locked up in overstock.



This is not a traditional role focused on building large teams; it is for a hands-on expert who looks at messy, fragmented systems and thinks, "This is fixable—I can take control". You will own the "just-in-time" flow of inventory to maximize cash flexibility and significantly reduce COGS.



What You Will Do

- Own Inventory Planning: Continuously monitor and manage inventory levels across Extensiv, WMS, and spreadsheets based on approved business logic.

- Strategic Forecasting: Use math-based equations to calculate 90-day rates of sale and production timelines (often 90 days) so purchase orders land exactly when needed.

- Maintain Site & Stock Integrity: Establish and validate min/max inventory levels, ensuring an "event minimum" is always on hand while adhering to investment budgets.

- 3PL & Fulfillment Ownership: Own the day-to-day relationship with 3PL partners, pushing them on performance, accuracy, and cost to ensure smooth inbound and outbound flows.

- Logistics & Cost Management: Lead cost reduction efforts across freight and logistics; negotiate with carriers (DHL, FedEx, USPS) to drive shipping costs toward optimized targets.

- Strategic "Alert" Reporting: Act as a watchdog; proactively identify and flag risks—such as over-ordering or cost leaks—before leadership sees them.

- SKU Rationalization: Drive the simplification of the business by meaningfully decreasing the SKU count.



You might be a good fit for us if...

- You Are a Spreadsheet Expert: You are highly hands-on and comfortable doing the heavy lifting in Excel and operational analysis.

- You Are Executive Facing: You can present data-backed pushback and tradeoffs (cash vs. inventory vs. availability) clearly to leadership.

- You Act with Directional Accuracy: You can operate in messy, imperfect systems without getting stuck, focusing on outcomes over perfect data.

- You Are a Strategic Interrogator: You don't just "take orders" or purchase because a system told you to; you challenge assumptions and identify issues early.

- You Are Low Ego & Direct: You are comfortable having difficult conversations and pushing back with recommendations rather than just questions.



Application Instructions: We value attention to detail above all else. When applying, please set your subject line to: [Your Name] | Distilled / Inventory & Operations Specialist. This helps us filter out automated applications and get straight to the top candidates.

-Coding transactions from bank feeds

-Expense and classes allocations

-Income and Sales entries

-Journal entries

-familiar with Canadian provincial tax

-Project and class allocations in Quickbooks online

-Financial Statements-P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow-Must be able to explain each one and what they do

-Handle numerous clients



We work in a fast paced industry so it is really important that you have

-2 years previous Full cycle bookkeeping experience with Quickbooks

-Able to work 8-9 hours a day within our time zone.

-Fast internet speed

-Computer and private work space

-Provide previous work references that can be contacted



When applying use the Header "I am your next Bookkeeper"

title: Digital Marketing & Lead Generation Specialist (SEO, Website, Email)
description:
Who We Are

Marketplace Insurance Exchange is a mission-driven organization focused on expanding access to insurance and improving how individuals and communities connect with coverage and resources.



We simplify complex systems, build strong networks, and drive initiatives that create real impact. As we grow, we are investing in digital strategies to increase awareness, engagement, and lead generation.



About the Role

We are looking for a results-driven Digital Marketing & Lead Generation Specialist whose primary goal is: Generate consistent, high-quality leads.



This is not just a marketing role. You will build, execute, and optimize funnels that turn traffic into real inquiries, sign-ups, and opportunities.



If you think in funnels, conversions, and measurable results (not just tasks), we want to work with you.



Key Responsibilities

  1. Plan and execute lead generation strategies

  2. Build and optimize funnels (traffic ? landing page ? lead ? follow-up)

  3. Improve conversion rates

  4. Track and report on:

            > Leads generated

            > Conversion rates

            > Campaign performance



What You’ll Be Doing

  1. Lead Generation & Digital Marketing 

           > Manage Google Ads campaigns for insurance clients and agent recruitment

           > Create and manage
/Instagram ads

           > Monitor and optimize performance (CTR, CPC, conversions, ROI)

           > Launch seasonal campaigns (e.g., Open Enrollment, Medicare, recruitment)

           > Track lead sources and performance

           > Identify new opportunities to generate leads

  2. Website & Landing Pages

           > Build and optimize landing pages and lead capture forms

           > Ensure tracking, forms, and integrations are working properly

           > Update website content (pages, blogs, images, services)

           > Maintain a professional, on-brand website

  3. SEO (Lead-Focused)

           > Conduct keyword research based on search intent

           > Optimize website content and pages

           > Support organic traffic growth that converts into leads

  4.
 Marketing

           > Create and send
 campaigns, newsletters, and announcements

           > Build simple automated follow-ups and drip sequences

           > Manage
 lists and segmentation

           > Track and improve open rates, clicks, and conversions

5. Graphic Design 

       Create visuals for:

           > Social media

           >
 campaigns

           > Website banners

           > Infographics

6. Light Recruiting Support

           > Post job ads

           > Organize applicants



Requirements

1. Proven experience in digital marketing and lead generation

2. Strong understanding of:

3. Landing pages & conversion optimization

4. SEO

5.
 marketing

6. Hands-on experience with Google Ads and/or Meta Ads

7. Experience with website platforms (WordPress or similar)

8. Design skills (Canva or similar)

9. Data-driven and results-oriented



Nice to Have

1. Experience with CRM or marketing tools

2. Funnel-building experience

3. Background in insurance, financial services, or professional services

4. Familiarity with tools like Canva, CapCut, or AI tools

5. Google Ads or Meta certifications



How We Measure Success

1. Number of leads generated

2. Conversion rates (landing pages & campaigns)

3.
 engagement & conversions

4. Growth in qualified traffic



This Role Is For You If

- You focus on results, not just tasks

- You understand lead generation and enjoy optimizing performance

- You are proactive and take ownership of your work

- You are comfortable wearing multiple hats in a growing team



Work Setup

Full-time: 40 hours per week

Schedule: US Central Time (CST)

Location: Remote



What We Offer

- Competitive pay based on experience

- Full-time, stable remote role

- Opportunity to own and grow the company’s marketing system

- Access to tools and budget to execute campaigns

- Supportive and collaborative team



How to Apply

1. A short introduction about your experience

2. Answer the following:

      > What is the most successful lead generation campaign you managed? Please include metrics (leads generated, conversion rates, or ROI)?

     > Which landing page tools or platforms have you used to optimize lead capture?

     > Describe one SEO or digital tactic you used specifically to generate qualified leads.   

     > Your favorite marketing tool and why?

3. An example of a lead generation campaign or funnel you worked on (Include your role and results).

4. Your portfolio link

5. Your CV 

6. Include the word “AMAZING” in your subject line
